/* end of the line */\n489:   if(multi->easyp) {\n490:     struct Curl_easy *last = multi->easylp;\n491:     last->next = data;\n492:     data->prev = last;\n493:     multi->easylp = data; /* the new last node */\n494:   }\n495:   else {\n496:     /* first node, make prev NULL! */\n497:     data->prev = NULL;\n498:     multi->easylp = multi->easyp = data; /* both first and last */\n499:   }\n500: }\n501: \n502: /* unlink the given easy handle from the linked list of easy handles */\n503: static void unlink_easy(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n504:                         struct Curl_easy *data)\n505: {\n506:   /* make the previous node point to our next */\n507:   if(data->prev)\n508:     data->prev->next = data->next;\n509:   else\n510:     multi->easyp = data->next; /* point to first node */\n511: \n512:   /* make our next point to our previous node */\n513:   if(data->next)\n514:     data->next->prev = data->prev;\n515:   else\n516:     multi->easylp = data->prev; /* point to last node */\n517: \n518:   data->prev = data->next = NULL;\n519: }\n520: \n521: \n522: CURLMcode curl_multi_add_handle(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n523:                                 struct Curl_easy *data)\n524: {\n525:   CURLMcode rc;\n526:   /* First, make some basic checks that the CURLM handle is a good handle */\n527:   if(!GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE(multi))\n528:     return CURLM_BAD_HANDLE;\n529: \n530:   /* Verify that we got a somewhat good easy handle too */\n531:   if(!GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(data))\n532:     return CURLM_BAD_EASY_HANDLE;\n533: \n534:   /* Prevent users from adding same easy handle more than once and prevent\n535:      adding to more than one multi stack */\n536:   if(data->multi)\n537:     return CURLM_ADDED_ALREADY;\n538: \n539:   if(multi->in_callback)\n540:     return CURLM_RECURSIVE_API_CALL;\n541: \n542:   if(multi->dead) {\n543:     /* a \"dead\" handle cannot get added transfers while any existing easy\n544:        handles are still alive - but if there are none alive anymore, it is\n545:        fine to start over and unmark the \"deadness\" of this handle */\n546:     if(multi->num_alive)\n547:       return CURLM_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK;\n548:     multi->dead = FALSE;\n549:   }\n550: \n551:   /* Initialize timeout list for this handle */\n552:   Curl_llist_init(&data->state.timeoutlist, NULL);\n553: \n554:   /*\n555:    * No failure allowed in this function beyond this point. And no\n556:    * modification of easy nor multi handle allowed before this except for\n557:    * potential multi's connection cache growing which won't be undone in this\n558:    * function no matter what.\n559:    */\n560:   if(data->set.errorbuffer)\n561:     data->set.errorbuffer[0] = 0;\n562: \n563:   /* make the Curl_easy refer back to this multi handle - before Curl_expire()\n564:      is called. */\n565:   data->multi = multi;\n566: \n567:   /* Set the timeout for this handle to expire really soon so that it will\n568:      be taken care of even when this handle is added in the midst of operation\n569:      when only the curl_multi_socket() API is used. During that flow, only\n570:      sockets that time-out or have actions will be dealt with. Since this\n571:      handle has no action yet, we make sure it times out to get things to\n572:      happen. */\n573:   Curl_expire(data, 0, EXPIRE_RUN_NOW);\n574: \n575:   /* A somewhat crude work-around for a little glitch in Curl_update_timer()\n576:      that happens if the lastcall time is set to the same time when the handle\n577:      is removed as when the next handle is added, as then the check in\n578:      Curl_update_timer() that prevents calling the application multiple times\n579:      with the same timer info will not trigger and then the new handle's\n580:      timeout will not be notified to the app.\n581: \n582:      The work-around is thus simply to clear the 'lastcall' variable to force\n583:      Curl_update_timer() to always trigger a callback to the app when a new\n584:      easy handle is added */\n585:   memset(&multi->timer_lastcall, 0, sizeof(multi->timer_lastcall));\n586: \n587:   rc = Curl_update_timer(multi);\n588:   if(rc)\n589:     return rc;\n590: \n591:   /* set the easy handle */\n592:   multistate(data, MSTATE_INIT);\n593: \n594:   /* for multi interface connections, we share DNS cache automatically if the\n595:      easy handle's one is currently not set. */\n596:   if(!data->dns.hostcache ||\n597:      (data->dns.hostcachetype == HCACHE_NONE)) {\n598:     data->dns.hostcache = &multi->hostcache;\n599:     data->dns.hostcachetype = HCACHE_MULTI;\n600:   }\n601: \n602:   /* Point to the shared or multi handle connection cache */\n603:   if(data->share && (data->share->specifier & (1<< CURL_LOCK_DATA_CONNECT)))\n604:     data->state.conn_cache = &data->share->conn_cache;\n605:   else\n606:     data->state.conn_cache = &multi->conn_cache;\n607:   data->state.lastconnect_id = -1;\n608: \n609: #ifdef USE_LIBPSL\n610:   /* Do the same for PSL. */\n611:   if(data->share && (data->share->specifier & (1 << CURL_LOCK_DATA_PSL)))\n612:     data->psl = &data->share->psl;\n613:   else\n614:     data->psl = &multi->psl;\n615: #endif\n616: \n617:   link_easy(multi, data);\n618: \n619:   /* increase the node-counter */\n620:   multi->num_easy++;\n621: \n622:   /* increase the alive-counter */\n623:   multi->num_alive++;\n624: \n625:   CONNCACHE_LOCK(data);\n626:   /* The closure handle only ever has default timeouts set. To improve the\n627:      state somewhat we clone the timeouts from each added handle so that the\n628:      closure handle always has the same timeouts as the most recently added\n629:      easy handle. */\n630:   data->state.conn_cache->closure_handle->set.timeout = data->set.timeout;\n631:   data->state.conn_cache->closure_handle->set.server_response_timeout =\n632:     data->set.server_response_timeout;\n633:   data->state.conn_cache->closure_handle->set.no_signal =\n634:     data->set.no_signal;\n635:   data->id = data->state.conn_cache->next_easy_id++;\n636:   if(data->state.conn_cache->next_easy_id <= 0)\n637:     data->state.conn_cache->next_easy_id = 0;\n638:   CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\n639: \n640:   multi_warn_debug(multi, data);\n641: \n642:   return CURLM_OK;\n643: }\n644: \n645: #if 0\n646: /* Debug-function, used like this:\n647:  *\n648:  * Curl_hash_print(&multi->sockhash, debug_print_sock_hash);\n649:  *\n650:  * Enable the hash print function first by editing hash.c\n651:  */\n652: static void debug_print_sock_hash(void *p)\n653: {\n654:   struct Curl_sh_entry *sh = (struct Curl_sh_entry *)p;\n655: \n656:   fprintf(stderr, \" [readers %u][writers %u]\",\n657:           sh->readers, sh->writers);\n658: }\n659: #endif\n660: \n661: static CURLcode multi_done(struct Curl_easy *data,\n662:                            CURLcode status,  /* an error if this is called\n663:                                                 after an error was detected */\n664:                            bool premature)\n665: {\n666:   CURLcode result;\n667:   struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n668: \n669: #if defined(DEBUGBUILD) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS)\n670:   DEBUGF(infof(data, \"multi_done[%s]: status: %d prem: %d done: %d\",\n671:                multi_statename[data->mstate],\n672:                (int)status, (int)premature, data->state.done));\n673: #else\n674:   DEBUGF(infof(data, \"multi_done: status: %d prem: %d done: %d\",\n675:                (int)status, (int)premature, data->state.done));\n676: #endif\n677: \n678:   if(data->state.done)\n679:     /* Stop if multi_done() has already been called */\n680:     return CURLE_OK;\n681: \n682:   /* Stop the resolver and free its own resources (but not dns_entry yet). */\n683:   Curl_resolver_kill(data);\n684: \n685:   /* Cleanup possible redirect junk */\n686:   Curl_safefree(data->req.newurl);\n687:   Curl_safefree(data->req.location);\n688: \n689:   switch(status) {\n690:   case CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK:\n691:   case CURLE_READ_ERROR:\n692:   case CURLE_WRITE_ERROR:\n693:     /* When we're aborted due to a callback return code it basically have to\n694:        be counted as premature as there is trouble ahead if we don't. We have\n695:        many callbacks and protocols work differently, we could potentially do\n696:        this more fine-grained in the future. */\n697:     premature = TRUE;\n698:   default:\n699:     break;\n700:   }\n701: \n702:   /* this calls the protocol-specific function pointer previously set */\n703:   if(conn->handler->done)\n704:     result = conn->handler->done(data, status, premature);\n705:   else\n706:     result = status;\n707: \n708:   if(CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK != result) {\n709:     /* avoid this if we already aborted by callback to avoid this calling\n710:        another callback */\n711:     int rc = Curl_pgrsDone(data);\n712:     if(!result && rc)\n713:       result = CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK;\n714:   }\n715: \n716:   /* Inform connection filters that this transfer is done */\n717:   Curl_conn_ev_data_done(data, premature);\n718: \n719:   process_pending_handles(data->multi); /* connection / multiplex */\n720: \n721:   Curl_safefree(data->state.ulbuf);\n722: \n723:   Curl_client_cleanup(data);\n724: \n725:   CONNCACHE_LOCK(data);\n726:   Curl_detach_connection(data);\n727:   if(CONN_INUSE(conn)) {\n728:     /* Stop if still used. */\n729:     CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\n730:     DEBUGF(infof(data, \"Connection still in use %zu, \"\n731:                  \"no more multi_done now!\",\n732:                  conn->easyq.size));\n733:     return CURLE_OK;\n734:   }\n735: \n736:   data->state.done = TRUE; /* called just now! */\n737: \n738:   if(conn->dns_entry) {\n739:     Curl_resolv_unlock(data, conn->dns_entry); /* done with this */\n740:     conn->dns_entry = NULL;\n741:   }\n742:   Curl_hostcache_prune(data);\n743: \n744:   /* if data->set.reuse_forbid is TRUE, it means the libcurl client has\n745:      forced us to close this connection. This is ignored for requests taking\n746:      place in a NTLM/NEGOTIATE authentication handshake\n747: \n748:      if conn->bits.close is TRUE, it means that the connection should be\n749:      closed in spite of all our efforts to be nice, due to protocol\n750:      restrictions in our or the server's end\n751: \n752:      if premature is TRUE, it means this connection was said to be DONE before\n753:      the entire request operation is complete and thus we can't know in what\n754:      state it is for reusing, so we're forced to close it. In a perfect world\n755:      we can add code that keep track of if we really must close it here or not,\n756:      but currently we have no such detail knowledge.\n757:   */\n758: \n759:   data->state.recent_conn_id = conn->connection_id;\n760:   if((data->set.reuse_forbid\n761: #if defined(USE_NTLM)\n762:       && !(conn->http_ntlm_state == NTLMSTATE_TYPE2 ||\n763:            conn->proxy_ntlm_state == NTLMSTATE_TYPE2)\n764: #endif\n765: #if defined(USE_SPNEGO)\n766:       && !(conn->http_negotiate_state == GSS_AUTHRECV ||\n767:            conn->proxy_negotiate_state == GSS_AUTHRECV)\n768: #endif\n769:      ) || conn->bits.close\n770:        || (premature && !Curl_conn_is_multiplex(conn, FIRSTSOCKET))) {\n771:     DEBUGF(infof(data, \"multi_done, not reusing connection=%\"\n772:                        CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T \", forbid=%d\"\n773:                        \", close=%d, premature=%d, conn_multiplex=%d\",\n774:                  conn->connection_id,\n775:                  data->set.reuse_forbid, conn->bits.close, premature,\n776:                  Curl_conn_is_multiplex(conn, FIRSTSOCKET)));\n777:     connclose(conn, \"disconnecting\");\n778:     Curl_conncache_remove_conn(data, conn, FALSE);\n779:     CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\n780:     Curl_disconnect(data, conn, premature);\n781:   }\n782:   else {\n783:     char buffer[256];\n784:     const char *host =\n785: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n786:       conn->bits.socksproxy ?\n787:       conn->socks_proxy.host.dispname :\n788:       conn->bits.httpproxy ? conn->http_proxy.host.dispname :\n789: #endif\n790:       conn->bits.conn_to_host ? conn->conn_to_host.dispname :\n791:       conn->host.dispname;\n792:     /* create string before returning the connection */\n793:     curl_off_t connection_id = conn->connection_id;\n794:     msnprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer),\n795:               \"Connection #%\" CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T \" to host %s left intact\",\n796:               connection_id, host);\n797:     /* the connection is no longer in use by this transfer */\n798:     CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\n799:     if(Curl_conncache_return_conn(data, conn)) {\n800:       /* remember the most recently used connection */\n801:       data->state.lastconnect_id = connection_id;\n802:       data->state.recent_conn_id = connection_id;\n803:       infof(data, \"%s\", buffer);\n804:     }\n805:     else\n806:       data->state.lastconnect_id = -1;\n807:   }\n808: \n809:   Curl_safefree(data->state.buffer);\n810:   return result;\n811: }\n812: \n813: static int close_connect_only(struct Curl_easy *data,\n814:                               struct connectdata *conn, void *param)\n815: {\n816:   (void)param;\n817:   if(data->state.lastconnect_id != conn->connection_id)\n818:     return 0;\n819: \n820:   if(!conn->connect_only)\n821:     return 1;\n822: \n823:   connclose(conn, \"Removing connect-only easy handle\");\n824: \n825:   return 1;\n826: }\n827: \n828: CURLMcode curl_multi_remove_handle(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n829:                                    struct Curl_easy *data)\n830: {\n831:   struct Curl_easy *easy = data;\n832:   bool premature;\n833:   struct Curl_llist_element *e;\n834:   CURLMcode rc;\n835: \n836:   /* First, make some basic checks that the CURLM handle is a good handle */\n837:   if(!GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE(multi))\n838:     return CURLM_BAD_HANDLE;\n839: \n840:   /* Verify that we got a somewhat good easy handle too */\n841:   if(!GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(data))\n842:     return CURLM_BAD_EASY_HANDLE;\n843: \n844:   /* Prevent users from trying to remove same easy handle more than once */\n845:   if(!data->multi)\n846:     return CURLM_OK; /* it is already removed so let's say it is fine! */\n847: \n848:   /* Prevent users from trying to remove an easy handle from the wrong multi */\n849:   if(data->multi != multi)\n850:     return CURLM_BAD_EASY_HANDLE;\n851: \n852:   if(multi->in_callback)\n853:     return CURLM_RECURSIVE_API_CALL;\n854: \n855:   premature = (data->mstate < MSTATE_COMPLETED) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n856: \n857:   /* If the 'state' is not INIT or COMPLETED, we might need to do something\n858:      nice to put the easy_handle in a good known state when this returns. */\n859:   if(premature) {\n860:     /* this handle is \"alive\" so we need to count down the total number of\n861:        alive connections when this is removed */\n862:     multi->num_alive--;\n863:   }\n864: \n865:   if(data->conn &&\n866:      data->mstate > MSTATE_DO &&\n867:      data->mstate < MSTATE_COMPLETED) {\n868:     /* Set connection owner so that the DONE function closes it.  We can\n869:        safely do this here since connection is killed. */\n870:     streamclose(data->conn, \"Removed with partial response\");\n871:   }\n872: \n873:   if(data->conn) {\n874:     /* multi_done() clears the association between the easy handle and the\n875:        connection.\n876: \n877:        Note that this ignores the return code simply because there's\n878:        nothing really useful to do with it anyway! */\n879:     (void)multi_done(data, data->result, premature);\n880:   }\n881: \n882:   /* The timer must be shut down before data->multi is set to NULL, else the\n883:      timenode will remain in the splay tree after curl_easy_cleanup is\n884:      called. Do it after multi_done() in case that sets another time! */\n885:   Curl_expire_clear(data);\n886: \n887:   if(data->connect_queue.ptr) {\n888:     /* the handle is in the pending or msgsent lists, so go ahead and remove\n889:        it */\n890:     if(data->mstate == MSTATE_PENDING)\n891:       Curl_llist_remove(&multi->pending, &data->connect_queue, NULL);\n892:     else\n893:       Curl_llist_remove(&multi->msgsent, &data->connect_queue, NULL);\n894:   }\n895:   if(in_main_list(data))\n896:     unlink_easy(multi, data);\n897: \n898:   if(data->dns.hostcachetype == HCACHE_MULTI) {\n899:     /* stop using the multi handle's DNS cache, *after* the possible\n900:        multi_done() call above */\n901:     data->dns.hostcache = NULL;\n902:     data->dns.hostcachetype = HCACHE_NONE;\n903:   }\n904: \n905:   Curl_wildcard_dtor(&data->wildcard);\n906: \n907:   /* change state without using multistate(), only to make singlesocket() do\n908:      what we want */\n909:   data->mstate = MSTATE_COMPLETED;\n910: \n911:   /* This ignores the return code even in case of problems because there's\n912:      nothing more to do about that, here */\n913:   (void)singlesocket(multi, easy); /* to let the application know what sockets\n914:                                       that vanish with this handle */\n915: \n916:   /* Remove the association between the connection and the handle */\n917:   Curl_detach_connection(data);\n918: \n919:   if(data->set.connect_only && !data->multi_easy) {\n920:     /* This removes a handle that was part the multi interface that used\n921:        CONNECT_ONLY, that connection is now left alive but since this handle\n922:        has bits.close set nothing can use that transfer anymore and it is\n923:        forbidden from reuse. And this easy handle cannot find the connection\n924:        anymore once removed from the multi handle\n925: \n926:        Better close the connection here, at once.\n927:     */\n928:     struct connectdata *c;\n929:     curl_socket_t s;\n930:     s = Curl_getconnectinfo(data, &c);\n931:     if((s != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) && c) {\n932:       Curl_conncache_remove_conn(data, c, TRUE);\n933:       Curl_disconnect(data, c, TRUE);\n934:     }\n935:   }\n936: \n937:   if(data->state.lastconnect_id != -1) {\n938:     /* Mark any connect-only connection for closure */\n939:     Curl_conncache_foreach(data, data->state.conn_cache,\n940:                            NULL, close_connect_only);\n941:   }\n942: \n943: #ifdef USE_LIBPSL\n944:   /* Remove the PSL association. */\n945:   if(data->psl == &multi->psl)\n946:     data->psl = NULL;\n947: #endif\n948: \n949:   /* as this was using a shared connection cache we clear the pointer to that\n950:      since we're not part of that multi handle anymore */\n951:   data->state.conn_cache = NULL;\n952: \n953:   data->multi = NULL; /* clear the association to this multi handle */\n954: \n955:   /* make sure there's no pending message in the queue sent from this easy\n956:      handle */\n957:   for(e = multi->msglist.head; e; e = e->next) {\n958:     struct Curl_message *msg = e->ptr;\n959: \n960:     if(msg->extmsg.easy_handle == easy) {\n961:       Curl_llist_remove(&multi->msglist, e, NULL);\n962:       /* there can only be one from this specific handle */\n963:       break;\n964:     }\n965:   }\n966: \n967:   /* NOTE NOTE NOTE\n968:      We do not touch the easy handle here! */\n969:   multi->num_easy--; /* one less to care about now */\n970: \n971:   process_pending_handles(multi);\n972: \n973:   rc = Curl_update_timer(multi);\n974:   if(rc)\n975:     return rc;\n976:   return CURLM_OK;\n977: }\n978: \n979: /* Return TRUE if the application asked for multiplexing */\n980: bool Curl_multiplex_wanted(const struct Curl_multi *multi)\n981: {\n982:   return (multi && (multi->multiplexing));\n983: }\n984: \n985: /*\n986:  * Curl_detach_connection() removes the given transfer from the connection.\n987:  *\n988:  * This is the only function that should clear data->conn. This will\n989:  * occasionally be called with the data->conn pointer already cleared.\n990:  */\n991: void Curl_detach_connection(struct Curl_easy *data)\n992: {\n993:   struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n994:   if(conn) {\n995:     Curl_conn_ev_data_detach(conn, data);\n996:     Curl_llist_remove(&conn->easyq, &data->conn_queue, NULL);\n997:   }\n998:   data->conn = NULL;\n999: }\n1000: \n1001: /*\n1002:  * Curl_attach_connection() attaches this transfer to this connection.\n1003:  *\n1004:  * This is the only function that should assign data->conn\n1005:  */\n1006: void Curl_attach_connection(struct Curl_easy *data,\n1007:                              struct connectdata *conn)\n1008: {\n1009:   DEBUGASSERT(!data->conn);\n1010:   DEBUGASSERT(conn);\n1011:   data->conn = conn;\n1012:   Curl_llist_insert_next(&conn->easyq, conn->easyq.tail, data,\n1013:                          &data->conn_queue);\n1014:   if(conn->handler && conn->handler->attach)\n1015:     conn->handler->attach(data, conn);\n1016:   Curl_conn_ev_data_attach(conn, data);\n1017: }\n1018: \n1019: static int domore_getsock(struct Curl_easy *data,\n1020:                           struct socks);\n1025:   return GETSOCK_BLANK;\n1026: }\n1027: \n1028: static int doing_getsock(struct Curl_easy *data,\n1029:                          struct connectdata *conn,\n1030:                          curl_socket_t *socks)\n1031: {\n1032:   if(conn && conn->handler->doing_getsock)\n1033:     return conn->handler->doing_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n1034:   return GETSOCK_BLANK;\n1035: }\n1036: \n1037: static int protocol_getsock(struct Curl_easy *data,\n1038:                             struct connectdata *conn,\n1039:                             curl_socket_t *socks)\n1040: {\n1041:   if(conn->handler->proto_getsock)\n1042:     return conn->handler->proto_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n1043:   return Curl_conn_get_select_socks(data, FIRSTSOCKET, socks);\n1044: }\n1045: \n1046: /* returns bitmapped flags for this handle and its sockets. The 'socks[]'\n1047:    array contains MAX_SOCKSPEREASYHANDLE entries. */\n1048: static int multi_getsock(struct Curl_easy *data,\n1049:                          curl_socket_t *socks)\n1050: {\n1051:   struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n1052:   /* The no connection case can happen when this is called from\n1053:      curl_multi_remove_handle() => singlesocket() => multi_getsock().\n1054:   */\n1055:   if(!conn)\n1056:     return 0;\n1057: \n1058:   switch(data->mstate) {\n1059:   default:\n1060:     return 0;\n1061: \n1062:   case MSTATE_RESOLVING:\n1063:     return Curl_resolv_getsock(data, socks);\n1064: \n1065:   case MSTATE_PROTOCONNECTING:\n1066:   case MSTATE_PROTOCONNECT:\n1067:     return protocol_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n1068: \n1069:   case MSTATE_DO:\n1070:   case MSTATE_DOING:\n1071:     return doing_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n1072: \n1073:   case MSTATE_TUNNELING:\n1074:   case MSTATE_CONNECTING:\n1075:     return Curl_conn_get_select_socks(data, FIRSTSOCKET, socks);\n1076: \n1077:   case MSTATE_DOING_MORE:\n1078:     return domore_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n1079: \n1080:   case MSTATE_DID: /* since is set after DO is completed, we switch to\n1081:                         waiting for the same as the PERFORMING state */\n1082:   case MSTATE_PERFORMING:\n1083:     return Curl_single_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n1084:   }\n1085: \n1086: }\n1087: \n1088: CURLMcode curl_multi_fdset(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n1089:                            fd_set *read_fd_set, fd_set *write_fd_set,\n1090:                            fd_set *exc_fd_set, int *max_fd)\n1091: {\n1092:   /* Scan through all the easy handles to get the file descriptors set.\n1093:      Some easy handles may not have connected to the remote host yet,\n1094:      and then we must make sure that is done. */\n1095:   struct Curl_easy *data;\n1096:   int this_max_fd = -1;\n1097:   curl_socket_t sockbunch[MAX_SOCKSPEREASYHANDLE];\n1098:   int i;\n1099:   (void)exc_fd_set; /* not used */\n1100: \n1101:   if(!GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE(multi))\n1102:     return CURLM_BAD_HANDLE;\n1103: \n1104:   if(multi->in_callback)\n1105:     return CURLM_RECURSIVE_API_CALL;\n1106: \n1107:   for(data = multi->easyp; #endif\n1258:             ufd->events |= POLLIN;\n1259:           }\n1260:           if(bitmap & GETSOCK_WRITESOCK(i)) {\n1261: #ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n1262:             mask |= #endif\n1320: \n1321: #if defined(ENABLE_WAKEUP) && defined(USE_WINSOCK)\n1322:   if(nfds || use_wakeup) {\n1323: #else\n1324:   if(nfds) {\n1325: #endif\n1326:     int pollrc;\n1327: #ifdef not be the same as the ones in the public libcurl API! */\n1352:       for(i = 0; i < extra_nfds; i++) {\n1353:         unsigned r = ufds[curlfds + i].revents;\n1354:         unsigned short mask = 0;\n1355: #ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n1356:         curl_socket_t s = extra_fds[i].fd;\n1357:         wsa_events.lNetworkEvents = 0;\n1358:         if(WSAEnumNetworkEvents(s, NULL, &wsa_events) == 0) {\n1359:      }\n1400:             else {\n1401:               /* break on entry not checked for being readable or writable */\n1402:               break;\n1403:             }\n1404:           }\n1405: try to read\n1417:                data from it until it receives an error (except EINTR).\n1418:                In normal cases it will get EAGAIN or EWOULDBLOCK\n1419:                when there is no more data, breaking the loop. */\n1420:             nread = wakeup_read(multi->wakeup_pair[0], buf, sizeof(buf));\n1421:             if(nread <= 0) {\n1422:               if(nread < 0 && EINTR == SOCKERRNO)\n1423:                 continue;\n1424:               break;\n1425:             }\n1426:           }\n1427:           /* do not count the wakeup socket into the returned value */\n1428: timeout_ms)\n1450:         sleep_ms = timeout_ms;\n1451:       /* when there are no easy handles in the multi, this holds a -1\n1452:          timeout */\n1453:       else if(sleep_ms < 0)\n1454:         sleep_ms = timeout_ms;\n1455:       Curl_wait_ms(sleep_ms);\n1456:     }\n1457:   }\n1458: \n1459:   return CURLM_OK;\n1460: }\n1461: \n1462: CURLMcode curl_multi_wait(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n1463:                           struct curl_waitfd extra_fds[],\n1464:                           unsigned int extra_nfds,\n1465:                           int timeout_ms,\n1466: \n1482: CURLMcode curl_multi_wakeup(struct Curl_multi *multi)\n1483: {\n1484:   /* this function is usually called from another thread,\n1485:      it has to be careful only to access parts of the\n1486:      Curl_multi struct that are constant */\n1487: \n1488:   /* GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE can be safely called */\n1489:   if(!GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE(multi))\n1490:     return CURLM_BAD_HANDLE;\n1491: \n1492: #ifdef ENABLE_WAKEUP\n1493: #ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n1494:   if(WSASetEvent(multi->wsa_event))\n1495:     return CURLM_OK;\n1496: #else\n1497:   /* the wakeup_pair variable is only written during init and cleanup,\n1498:      making it safe to access from another thread after the init part\n1499:      and before cleanup */\n1500:   if(multi->wakeup_pair[1] != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) {\n1501:     char buf[1];\n1502:     buf[0] = 1;\n1503:     while(1) {\n1504:       /* swrite() is not thread-safe in general, because concurrent calls\n1505:          can have their messages interleaved, but in this case the content\n1506:          of the messages does not matter, which makes it ok to call.\n1507: \n1508:          The write socket is set to non-blocking, this way this function\n1509:          cannot block, making it safe to call even from the same thread\n1510:          that will call curl_multi_wait(). If swrite() returns that it\n1511:          would block, it's considered successful because it means that\n1512:          previous calls to this function will wake up the poll(). */\n1513:       if(wakeup_write(multi->wakeup_pair[1], buf, sizeof(buf)) < 0) {\n1514:         int err = SOCKERRNO;\n1515:         int return_success;\n1516: #ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n1517:         return_success = WSAEWOULDBLOCK == err;\n1518: #else\n1519:         if(EINTR == err)\n1520:           continue;\n1521:         return_success = EWOULDBLOCK == err || EAGAIN == err;\n1522: #endif\n1523:         if(!return_success)\n1524:           return CURLM_WAKEUP_FAILURE;\n1525:       }\n1526:       return CURLM_OK;\n1527:     }\n1528:   }\n1529: #endif\n1530: #endif\n1531:   return CURLM_WAKEUP_FAILURE;\n1532: }\n1533: \n1534: /*\n1535:  * multi_ischanged() is called\n1536:  *\n1537:  * Returns TRUE/FALSE whether the state is changed to trigger a CONNECT_PEND\n1538:  * => CONNECT action.\n1539:  *\n1540:  * Set 'clear' to TRUE to have it also clear the state variable.\n1541:  */\n1542: static bool multi_ischanged(struct Curl_multi *multi, bool clear)\n1543: {\n1544:   bool retval = multi->recheckstate;\n1545:   if(clear)\n1546:     multi->recheckstate = FALSE;\n1547:   return retval;\n1548: }\n1549: \n1550: /*\n1551:  * Curl_multi_connchanged() is called to tell that there is a connection in\n1552:  * this multi handle that has changed state (multiplexing become possible, the\n1553:  * number of allowed streams changed or similar), and a subsequent use of this\n1554:  * multi handle should move CONNECT_PEND handles back to CONNECT to have them\n1555:  * retry.\n1556:  */\n1557: void Curl_multi_connchanged(struct Curl_multi *multi)\n1558: {\n1559:   multi->recheckstate = TRUE;\n1560: }\n1561: \n1562: CURLMcode Curl_multi_add_perform(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n1563:                                  struct Curl_easy *data,\n1564:                                  struct connectdata *conn)\n1565: {\n1566:   CURLMcode rc;\n1567: \n1568:   if(multi->in_callback)\n1569:     return CURLM_RECURSIVE_API_CALL;\n1570: \n1571:   rc = curl_multi_add_handle(multi, data);\n1572:   if(!rc) {\n1573:     struct SingleRequest *k = &data->req;\n1574: \n1575:     /* pass in NULL for 'conn' here since we don't want to init the\n1576:        connection, only this transfer */\n1577:     Curl_init_do(data, NULL);\n1578: \n1579:     /* take this handle to the perform state right away */\n1580:     multistate(data, MSTATE_PERFORMING);\n1581:     Curl_attach_connection(data, conn);\n1582:     k->keepon |= KEEP_RECV; /* setup to receive! */\n1583:   }\n1584:   return rc;\n1585: }\n1586: \n1587: static CURLcode multi_do(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done)\n1588: {\n1589:   CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n1590:   struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n1591: \n1592:   DEBUGASSERT(conn);\n1593:   DEBUGASSERT(conn->handler);\n1594: \n1595:   if(conn->handler->do_it)\n1596:     result = conn->handler->do_it(data, done);\n1597: \n1598:   return result;\n1599: }\n1600: \n1601: /*\n1602:  * multi_do_more() is called during the DO_MORE multi state. It is basically a\n1603:  * second stage DO state which (wrongly) was introduced to support FTP's\n1604:  * second connection.\n1605:  *\n1606:  * 'complete' can return 0 for incomplete, 1 for done and -1 for go back to\n1607:  * DOING state there's more work to do!\n1608:  */\n1609: \n1610: static CURLcode multi_do_more(struct Curl_easy *data, int *complete)\n1611: {\n1612:   CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n1613:   struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n1614: \n1615:   *complete = 0;\n1616: \n1617:   if(conn->handler->do_more)\n1618:     result = conn->handler->do_more(data, complete);\n1619: \n1620:   return result;\n1621: }\n1622: \n1623: /*\n1624:  * Check whether a timeout occurred, and handle it if it did\n1625:  */\n1626: static bool multi_handle_timeout(struct Curl_easy *data,\n1627:                                  struct curltime *now,\n1628:                                  bool *stream_error,\n1629:                                  CURLcode *result,\n1630:                                  bool connect_timeout)\n1631: {\n1632:   timediff_t timeout_ms;\n1633:   timeout_ms = Curl_timeleft(data, now, connect_timeout);\n1634: \n1635:   if(timeout_ms < 0) {\n1636:     /* Handle timed out */\n1637:     if(data->mstate == MSTATE_RESOLVING)\n1638:       failf(data, \"Resolving timed out after %\" CURL_FORMAT_TIMEDIFF_T\n1639:             \" milliseconds\",\n1640:      }\n1654:       else {\n1655:         failf(data, \"Operation timed out after %\" CURL_FORMAT_TIMEDIFF_T\n1656:               \" milliseconds with %\" CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T\n1657:               \" bytes received\",\n1658:               Curl_timediff(*now, data->progress.t_startsingle),\n1659:               k->bytecount);\n1660:       }\n1661:     }\n1662: \n1663:     /* Force connection Still, every\\n77:   CURL handle takes 45-50 K memory, therefore this 3K are not significant.\\n78: */\\n79: #ifndef CURL_SOCKET_HASH_TABLE_SIZE\\n80: #define CURL_SOCKET_HASH_TABLE_SIZE 911\\n81: #endif\\n82: \\n83: #ifndef CURL_CONNECTION_HASH_SIZE\\n84: #define CURL_CONNECTION_HASH_SIZE 97\\n85: #endif\\n86: \\n87: #ifndef CURL_DNS_HASH_SIZE\\n88: #define CURL_DNS_HASH_SIZE 71\\n89: #endif\\n90: \\n91: #define CURL_MULTI_HANDLE 0x000bab1e\\n92: \\n93: #ifdef DEBUGBUILD\\n94: /* On a debug build, we want to fail hard on multi handles that\\n95:  * are not NULL, but no longer have the MAGIC touch. TRUE: \\\\\\n99:   (DEBUGASSERT(!(x)), FALSE))\\n100: #else\\n101: #define GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE(x) \\\\\\n102:   ((x) && (x)->magic == CURL_MULTI_HANDLE)\\n103: #endif\\n104: \\n105: static CURLMcode singlesocket(struct Curl_multi *multi,\\n106:                               struct Curl_easy *data);\\n107: static CURLMcode add_next_timeout(struct curltime now,\\n108:                                   struct Curl_multi *multi,\\n109:                                   struct Curl_easy *d);\\n110: static CURLMcode multi_timeout(struct Curl_multi *multi,\\n111:                                long *timeout_ms);\\n112: static void process_pending_handles(struct Curl_multi *multi);\\n113: \\n114: #ifdef DEBUGBUILD\\n115: static const char * const multi_statename[]={\\n116:   \\\"INIT\\\",\\n117:   \\\"PENDING\\\",\\n118:   \\\"CONNECT\\\",\\n119:   \\\"RESOLVING\\\",\\n120:   \\\"CONNECTING\\\",\\n121:   \\\"TUNNELING\\\",\\n122:   \\\"PROTOCONNECT\\\",\\n123:   \\\"PROTOCONNECTING\\\",\\n124:   \\\"DO\\\",\\n125:   \\\"DOING\\\",\\n126:   \\\"DOING_MORE\\\",\\n127:   \\\"DID\\\",\\n128:   \\\"PERFORMING\\\",\\n129:   \\\"RATELIMITING\\\",\\n130:   \\\"DONE\\\",\\n131:   \\\"COMPLETED\\\",\\n132:   \\\"MSGSENT\\\",\\n133: };\\n134: #endif\\n135: \\n136: /* function pointer called once when switching TO a state */\\n137: typedef void (*init_multistate_func)(struct Curl_easy *data);\\n138: \\n139: /* called in DID state, before PERFORMING state */\\n140: static void before_perform(struct Curl_easy *data)\\n141: {\\n142:   data->req.chunk = FALSE;\\n143:   Curl_pgrsTime(data, TIMER_PRETRANSFER);\\n144: }\\n145: \\n146: static void init_completed(struct Curl_easy *data)\\n147: {\\n148:   /* this is a completed transfer */\\n149: \\n150:   /* Important: reset the conn pointer so that we don't point to memory\\n151:      that could be freed anytime */\\n152:   Curl_detach_connection(data);\\n153:   Curl_expire_clear(data); /* stop all timers */\\n154: }\\n155: \\n156: /* always use this function to change state, to make debugging easier */\\n157: static void mstate(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLMstate state\\n158: #ifdef DEBUGBUILD\\n159:                    , int lineno\\n160: #endif\\n161: )\\n162: {\\n163:   CURLMstate oldstate = data->mstate;\\n164:   static const init_multistate_func finit[MSTATE_LAST] = {\\n165:     NULL,              /* INIT */\\n166:     NULL,              /* PENDING */\\n167:     Curl_init_CONNECT, /* CONNECT */\\n168:     NULL,              /* RESOLVING */\\n169:     NULL,              /* CONNECTING */\\n170:     NULL,              /* TUNNELING */\\n171:     NULL,              /* PROTOCONNECT */\\n172:     NULL,              /* PROTOCONNECTING */\\n173:     NULL,              /* DO */\\n174:     NULL,              /* DOING */\\n175:     NULL,              /* DOING_MORE */\\n176:     before_perform,    /* DID */\\n177:     NULL,              /* PERFORMING */\\n178:     NULL,              /* RATELIMITING */\\n179:     NULL,              /* DONE */\\n180:     init_completed,    /* COMPLETED */\\n181:     NULL               /* MSGSENT */\\n182:   };\\n183: \\n184: #if defined(DEBUGBUILD) && defined(CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS)\\n185:   (void) lineno;\\n186: #endif\\n187: \\n188:   if(oldstate == state)\\n189:     /* don't bother when the new state is the same as the old state */\\n190:     return;\\n191: \\n192:   data->mstate = state;\\n193: \\n194: #if defined(DEBUGBUILD) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS)\\n195:   if(data->mstate >= MSTATE_PENDING &&\\n196:      data->mstate < MSTATE_COMPLETED) {\\n197:     infof(data,\\n198:           \\\"STATE: %s => %s handle %p; line %d\\\",\\n199:           multi_statename[oldstate], multi_statename[data->mstate],\\n200:           (void *)data, lineno);\\n201:   }\\n202: #endif\\n203: \\n204:   if(state == MSTATE_COMPLETED) {\\n205:     /* changing to COMPLETED means there's one less easy handle 'alive' */\\n206:     DEBUGASSERT(data->multi->num_alive > 0);\\n207:     data->multi->num_alive--;\\n208:   }\\n209: \\n210:   /* if this state has an init-function, run it */\\n211:   if(finit[state])\\n212:     finit[state](data);\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: #ifndef DEBUGBUILD\\n216: #define multistate(x,y) mstate(x,y)\\n217: #else\\n218: #define multistate(x,y) mstate(x,y, __LINE__)\\n219: #endif\\n220: \\n221: /*\\n222:  * We add one of these structs to the sockhash for each socket\\n223:  */\\n224: \\n225: struct Curl_sh_entry {\\n226:   struct Curl_hash transfers; /* hash of transfers using this socket */\\n227:   unsigned int action;  /* what combined action READ/WRITE this socket waits\\n228:                            for */\\n229:   unsigned int users; /* number of transfers using this */\\n230:   void *socketp; /* settable by users with curl_multi_assign() */\\n231:   unsigned int readers; /* this many transfers want to read */\\n232:   unsigned int writers; /* this many transfers want to write */\\n233: };\\n234: /* bits for 'action' having no bits means this socket is not expecting any\\n235:    action */\\n236: #define SH_READ  1\\n237: #define SH_WRITE 2\\n238: \\n239: /* look up a given socket in the socket hash, skip invalid sockets */\\n240: static struct Curl_sh_entry *sh_getentry(struct Curl_hash *sh,\\n241:                                          curl_socket_t s)\\n242: {\\n243:   if(s != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) {\\n244:     /* only look for proper sockets */\\n245:     return Curl_hash_pick(sh, (char *)&s, sizeof(curl_socket_t));\\n246:   }\\n247:   return NULL;\\n248: }\\n249: \\n250: #define TRHASH_SIZE 13\\n251: static size_t trhash(void *key, size_t key_length, size_t slots_num)\\n252: {\\n253:   size_t keyval = (size_t)*(struct Curl_easy **)key;\\n254:   (void) key_length;\\n255: \\n256:   return (keyval % slots_num);\\n257: }\\n258: \\n259: static size_t trhash_compare(void *k1, size_t k1_len, void *k2, size_t k2_len)\\n260: {\\n261:   (void)k1_len;\\n262:   (void)k2_len;\\n263: \\n264:   return *(struct Curl_easy **)k1 == *(struct Curl_easy **)k2;\\n265: }\\n266: \\n267: static void trhash_dtor(void *nada)\\n268: {\\n269:   (void)nada;\\n270: }\\n271: \\n272: /*\\n273:  * The sockhash has its own separate subhash in each entry that need to be\\n274:  * safely destroyed first.\\n275:  */\\n276: static void sockhash_destroy(struct Curl_hash *h)\\n277: {\\n278:   struct Curl_hash_iterator iter;\\n279:   struct Curl_hash_element *he;\\n280: \\n281:   DEBUGASSERT(h);\\n282:   Curl_hash_start_iterate(h, &iter);\\n283:   he = Curl_hash_next_element(&iter);\\n284:   while(he) {\\n285:     struct Curl_sh_entry *sh = (struct Curl_sh_entry *)he->ptr;\\n286:     Curl_hash_destroy(&sh->transfers);\\n287:     he = Curl_hash_next_element(&iter);\\n288:   }\\n289:   Curl_hash_destroy(h);\\n290: }\\n291: \\n292: \\n293: /* make sure this socket is present in the hash for this handle */\\n294: static struct Curl_sh_entry *sh_addentry(struct Curl_hash *sh,\\n295:                                          curl_socket_t s)\\n296: {\\n297:   struct Curl_sh_entry *there = sh_getentry(sh, s);\\n298:   struct Curl_sh_entry *check;\\n299: \\n300:   if(there) {\\n301:     /* it is present, return fine */\\n302:     return there;\\n303:   }\\n304: \\n305:   /* not present, add it */\\n306:   check = calloc(1, sizeof(struct Curl_sh_entry));\\n307:   if(!check)\\n308:     return NULL; /* major failure */\\n309: \\n310:   Curl_hash_init(&check->transfers, TRHASH_SIZE, trhash, trhash_compare,\\n311:                  trhash_dtor);\\n312: \\n313:   /* make/add new hash entry */\\n314:   if(!Curl_hash_add(sh, (char *)&s, sizeof(curl_socket_t), check)) {\\n315:     Curl_hash_destroy(&check->transfers);\\n316:     free(check);\\n317:     return NULL; /* major failure */\\n318:   }\\n319: \\n320:   return check; /* things are good in sockhash land */\\n321: }\\n322: \\n323: \\n324: /* delete the given socket + handle from the hash */\\n325: static void sh_delentry(struct Curl_sh_entry *entry,\\n326:                         struct Curl_hash *sh, curl_socket_t s)\\n327: {\\n328:   Curl_hash_destroy(&entry->transfers);\\n329: \\n330:   /* We remove the hash entry. This will end up in a call to\\n331:      sh_freeentry(). */\\n332:   Curl_hash_delete(sh, (char *)&s, sizeof(curl_socket_t));\\n333: }\\n334: \\n335: /*\\n336:  * free a sockhash entry\\n337:  */\\n338: static void sh_freeentry(void *freethis)\\n339: {\\n340:   struct Curl_sh_entry *p = (struct Curl_sh_entry *) freethis;\\n341: \\n342:   free(p);\\n343: }\\n344: \\n345: static size_t fd_key_compare(void *k1, size_t k1_len, void *k2, size_t k2_len)\\n346: {\\n347:   (void) k1_len; (void) k2_len;\\n348: \\n349:   return (*((curl_socket_t *) k1)) == (*((curl_socket_t *) k2));\\n350: }\\n351: \\n352: static size_t hash_fd(void *key, size_t key_length, size_t slots_num)\\n353: {\\n354:   curl_socket_t fd = *((curl_socket_t *) key);\\n355:   (void) key_length;\\n356: \\n357:   return (fd % slots_num);\\n358: }\\n359: \\n360: /*\\n361:  * sh_init() creates a new socket hash and returns the handle for it.\\n362:  *\\n363:  * Quote from README.multi_socket:\\n364:  *\\n365:  * \\\"Some tests at 7000 and 9000 connections showed that the socket hash lookup\\n366:  * is somewhat of a bottle neck. Its current implementation may be a bit too\\n367:  * limiting. It simply has a fixed-size array, and on each entry in the array\\n368:  * it has a linked list with entries. So the hash only checks which list to\\n369:  * scan through. The code I had used so for used a list with merely 7 slots\\n370:  * (as that is what the DNS hash uses) but with 7000 connections that would\\n371:  * make an average of 1000 nodes in each list to run through. I upped that to\\n372:  * 97 slots (I believe a prime is suitable) and noticed a significant speed\\n373:  * increase.  I need to reconsider the hash implementation or use a rather\\n374:  * large default value like this. At 9000 connections I was still below 10us\\n375:  * per call.\\\"\\n376:  *\\n377:  */\\n378: static void sh_init(struct Curl_hash *hash, int hashsize)\\n379: {\\n380:   Curl_hash_init(hash, hashsize, hash_fd, fd_key_compare,\\n381:                  sh_freeentry);\\n382: }\\n383: \\n384: /*\\n385:  * multi_addmsg()\\n386:  *\\n387:  * Called when a transfer is completed. Adds the given msg pointer to\\n388:  * the list kept in the multi handle.\\n389:  */\\n390: static void multi_addmsg(struct Curl_multi *multi, struct Curl_message *msg)\\n391: {\\n392:   Curl_llist_insert_next(&multi->msglist, multi->msglist.tail, msg,\\n393:                          &msg->list);\\n394: }\\n395: \\n396: struct Curl_multi *Curl_multi_handle(int hashsize, /* socket hash */\\n397:                                      int chashsize, /* connection hash */\\n398:                                      int dnssize) /* dns hash */\\n399: {\\n400:   struct Curl_multi *multi = calloc(1, sizeof(struct Curl_multi));\\n401: \\n402:   if(!multi)\\n403:     return NULL;\\n404: \\n405:   multi->magic = CURL_MULTI_HANDLE;\\n406: \\n407:   Curl_init_dnscache(&multi->hostcache, dnssize);\\n408: \\n409:   sh_init(&multi->sockhash, hashsize);\\n410: \\n411:   if(Curl_conncache_init(&multi->conn_cache, chashsize))\\n412:     goto error;\\n413: \\n414:   Curl_llist_init(&multi->msglist, NULL);\\n415:   Curl_llist_init(&multi->pending, NULL);\\n416:   Curl_llist_init(&multi->msgsent, NULL);\\n417: \\n418:   multi->multiplexing = TRUE;\\n419: \\n420:   /* -1 means it not set by user, use the default value */\\n421:   multi->maxconnects = -1;\\n422:   multi->max_concurrent_streams = 100;\\n423: \\n424: #ifdef USE_WINSOCK\\n425:   multi->wsa_event = WSACreateEvent();\\n426:   if(multi->wsa_event == WSA_INVALID_EVENT)\\n427:     goto error;\\n428: #else\\n429: #ifdef ENABLE_WAKEUP\\n430:   if(wakeup_create(multi->wakeup_pair) < 0) {\\n431:     multi->wakeup_pair[0] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\\n432:     multi->wakeup_pair[1] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\\n433:   }\\n434:   else if(curlx_nonblock(multi->wakeup_pair[0], TRUE) < 0 ||\\n435:           curlx_nonblock(multi->wakeup_pair[1], TRUE) < 0) {\\n436:     wakeup_close(multi->wakeup_pair[0]);\\n437:     wakeup_close(multi->wakeup_pair[1]);\\n438:     multi->wakeup_pair[0] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\\n439:     multi->wakeup_pair[1] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\\n440:   }\\n441: #endif\\n442: #endif\\n443: \\n444:   return multi;\\n445: \\n446: error:\\n447: \\n448:   sockhash_destroy(&multi->sockhash);\\n449:   Curl_hash_destroy(&multi->hostcache);\\n450:   Curl_conncache_destroy(&multi->conn_cache);\\n451:   free(multi);\\n452:   return NULL;\\n453: }\\n454: \\n455: struct Curl_multi *curl_multi_init(void)\\n456: {\\n457:   return Curl_multi_handle(CURL_SOCKET_HASH_TABLE_SIZE,\\n458:                            CURL_CONNECTION_HASH_SIZE,\\n459:                            CURL_DNS_HASH_SIZE);\\n460: }\\n461: \\n462: #if defined(DEBUGBUILD) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS)\\n463: static void multi_warn_debug(struct Curl_multi *multi, struct Curl_easy *data)\\n464: {\\n465:   if(!multi->warned) {\\n466:     infof(data, \\\"!!! WARNING !!!\\\");\\n467:     infof(data, \\\"This is a debug build of libcurl, \\\"\\n468:           \\\"do not use in production.\\\");\\n469:     multi->warned = true;\\n470:   }\\n471: }\\n472: #else\\n473: #define multi_warn_debug(x,y) Curl_nop_stmt\\n474: #endif\\n475: \\n476: /* returns TRUE if the easy handle is supposed to be present in the main link\\n477:    list */\\n478: static bool in_main_list(struct Curl_easy *data)\\n479: {\\n480:   return ((data->mstate != MSTATE_PENDING) &&\\n481:           (data->mstate != MSTATE_MSGSENT));\\n482: }\\n483: \\n484: static void link_easy(struct Curl_multi *multi,\\n485:                       struct Curl_easy *data)\\n486: {\\n487:   /* We add the new easy entry last in the list. */\\n488:   data->next = NULL; /* end of the line */\\n489:   if(multi->easyp) {\\n490:     struct Curl_easy *last = multi->easylp;\\n491:     last->next = data;\\n492:     data->prev = last;\\n493:     multi->easylp = data; /* the new last node */\\n494:   }\\n495:   else {\\n496:     /* first node, make prev NULL! */\\n497:     data->prev = NULL;\\n498:     multi->easylp = multi->easyp = data; /* both first and last */\\n499:   }\\n500: }\\n501: \\n502: /* unlink the given easy handle from the linked list of easy handles */\\n503: static void unlink_easy(struct Curl_multi *multi,\\n504:                         struct Curl_easy *data)\\n505: {\\n506:   /* make the previous node point to our next */\\n507:   if(data->prev)\\n508:     data->prev->next = data->next;\\n509:   else\\n510:     multi->easyp = data->next; /* point to first node */\\n511: \\n512:   /* make our next point to our previous node */\\n513:   if(data->next)\\n514:     data->next->prev = data->prev;\\n515:   else\\n516:     multi->easylp = data->prev; /* point to last node */\\n517: \\n518:   data->prev = data->next = NULL;\\n519: }\\n520: \\n521: \\n522: CURLMcode curl_multi_add_handle(struct Curl_multi *multi,\\n523:                                 struct Curl_easy *data)\\n524: {\\n525:   CURLMcode rc;\\n526:   /* First, make some basic checks that the CURLM handle is a good handle */\\n527:   if(!GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE(multi))\\n528:     return CURLM_BAD_HANDLE;\\n529: \\n530:   /* Verify that we got a somewhat good easy handle too */\\n531:   if(!GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(data))\\n532:     return CURLM_BAD_EASY_HANDLE;\\n533: \\n534:   /* Prevent users from adding same easy handle more than once and prevent\\n535:      adding to more than one multi stack */\\n536:   if(data->multi)\\n537:     return CURLM_ADDED_ALREADY;\\n538: \\n539:   if(multi->in_callback)\\n540:     return CURLM_RECURSIVE_API_CALL;\\n541: \\n542:   if(multi->dead) {\\n543:     /* a \\\"dead\\\" handle cannot get added transfers while any existing easy\\n544:        handles are still alive - but if there are none alive anymore, it is\\n545:        fine to start over and unmark the \\\"deadness\\\" of this handle */\\n546:     if(multi->num_alive)\\n547:       return CURLM_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK;\\n548:     multi->dead = FALSE;\\n549:   }\\n550: \\n551:   /* Initialize timeout list for this handle */\\n552:   Curl_llist_init(&data->state.timeoutlist, NULL);\\n553: \\n554:   /*\\n555:    * No failure allowed in this function beyond this point. And no\\n556:    * modification of easy nor multi handle allowed before this except for\\n557:    * potential multi's connection cache growing which won't be undone in this\\n558:    * function no matter what.\\n559:    */\\n560:   if(data->set.errorbuffer)\\n561:     data->set.errorbuffer[0] = 0;\\n562: \\n563:   /* make the Curl_easy refer back to this multi handle - before Curl_expire()\\n564:      is called. */\\n565:   data->multi = multi;\\n566: \\n567:   /* Set the timeout for this handle to expire really soon so that it will\\n568:      be taken care of even when this handle is added in the midst of operation\\n569:      when only the curl_multi_socket() API is used. During that flow, only\\n570:      sockets that time-out or have actions will be dealt with. Since this\\n571:      handle has no action yet, we make sure it times out to get things to\\n572:      happen. */\\n573:   Curl_expire(data, 0, EXPIRE_RUN_NOW);\\n574: \\n575:   /* A somewhat crude work-around for a little glitch in Curl_update_timer()\\n576:      that happens if the lastcall time is set to the same time when the handle\\n577:      is removed as when the next handle is added, as then the check in\\n578:      Curl_update_timer() that prevents calling the application multiple times\\n579:      with the same timer info will not trigger and then the new handle's\\n580:      timeout will not be notified to the app.\\n581: \\n582:      The work-around is thus simply to clear the 'lastcall' variable to force\\n583:      Curl_update_timer() to always trigger a callback to the app when a new\\n584:      easy handle is added */\\n585:   memset(&multi->timer_lastcall, 0, sizeof(multi->timer_lastcall));\\n586: \\n587:   rc = Curl_update_timer(multi);\\n588:   if(rc)\\n589:     return rc;\\n590: \\n591:   /* set the easy handle */\\n592:   multistate(data, MSTATE_INIT);\\n593: \\n594:   /* for multi interface connections, we share DNS cache automatically if the\\n595:      easy handle's one is currently not set. */\\n596:   if(!data->dns.hostcache ||\\n597:      (data->dns.hostcachetype == HCACHE_NONE)) {\\n598:     data->dns.hostcache = &multi->hostcache;\\n599:     data->dns.hostcachetype = HCACHE_MULTI;\\n600:   }\\n601: \\n602:   /* Point to the shared or multi handle connection cache */\\n603:   if(data->share && (data->share->specifier & (1<< CURL_LOCK_DATA_CONNECT)))\\n604:     data->state.conn_cache = &data->share->conn_cache;\\n605:   else\\n606:     data->state.conn_cache = &multi->conn_cache;\\n607:   data->state.lastconnect_id = -1;\\n608: \\n609: #ifdef USE_LIBPSL\\n610:   /* Do the same for PSL. */\\n611:   if(data->share && (data->share->specifier & (1 << CURL_LOCK_DATA_PSL)))\\n612:     data->psl = &data->share->psl;\\n613:   else\\n614:     data->psl = &multi->psl;\\n615: #endif\\n616: \\n617:   link_easy(multi, data);\\n618: \\n619:   /* increase the node-counter */\\n620:   multi->num_easy++;\\n621: \\n622:   /* increase the alive-counter */\\n623:   multi->num_alive++;\\n624: \\n625:   CONNCACHE_LOCK(data);\\n626:   /* The closure handle only ever has default timeouts set. To improve the\\n627:      state somewhat we clone the timeouts from each added handle so that the\\n628:      closure handle always has the same timeouts as the most recently added\\n629:      easy handle. */\\n630:   data->state.conn_cache->closure_handle->set.timeout = data->set.timeout;\\n631:   data->state.conn_cache->closure_handle->set.server_response_timeout =\\n632:     data->set.server_response_timeout;\\n633:   data->state.conn_cache->closure_handle->set.no_signal =\\n634:     data->set.no_signal;\\n635:   data->id = data->state.conn_cache->next_easy_id++;\\n636:   if(data->state.conn_cache->next_easy_id <= 0)\\n637:     data->state.conn_cache->next_easy_id = 0;\\n638:   CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\\n639: \\n640:   multi_warn_debug(multi, data);\\n641: \\n642:   return CURLM_OK;\\n643: }\\n644: \\n645: #if 0\\n646: /* Debug-function, used like this:\\n647:  *\\n648:  * Curl_hash_print(&multi->sockhash, debug_print_sock_hash);\\n649:  *\\n650:  * Enable the hash print function first by editing hash.c\\n651:  */\\n652: static void debug_print_sock_hash(void *p)\\n653: {\\n654:   struct Curl_sh_entry *sh = (struct Curl_sh_entry *)p;\\n655: \\n656:   fprintf(stderr, \\\" [readers %u][writers %u]\\\",\\n657:           sh->readers, sh->writers);\\n658: }\\n659: #endif\\n660: \\n661: static CURLcode multi_done(struct Curl_easy *data,\\n662:                            CURLcode status,  /* an error if this is called\\n663:                                                 after an error was detected */\\n664:                            bool premature)\\n665: {\\n666:   CURLcode result;\\n667:   struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\\n668: \\n669: #if defined(DEBUGBUILD) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS)\\n670:   DEBUGF(infof(data, \\\"multi_done[%s]: status: %d prem: %d done: %d\\\",\\n671:                multi_statename[data->mstate],\\n672:                (int)status, (int)premature, data->state.done));\\n673: #else\\n674:   DEBUGF(infof(data, \\\"multi_done: status: %d prem: %d done: %d\\\",\\n675:                (int)status, (int)premature, data->state.done));\\n676: #endif\\n677: \\n678:   if(data->state.done)\\n679:     /* Stop if multi_done() has already been called */\\n680:     return CURLE_OK;\\n681: \\n682:   /* Stop the resolver and free its own resources (but not dns_entry yet). */\\n683:   Curl_resolver_kill(data);\\n684: \\n685:   /* Cleanup possible redirect junk */\\n686:   Curl_safefree(data->req.newurl);\\n687:   Curl_safefree(data->req.location);\\n688: \\n689:   switch(status) {\\n690:   case CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK:\\n691:   case CURLE_READ_ERROR:\\n692:   case CURLE_WRITE_ERROR:\\n693:     /* When we're aborted due to a callback return code it basically have to\\n694:        be counted as premature as there is trouble ahead if we don't. We have\\n695:        many callbacks and protocols work differently, we could potentially do\\n696:        this more fine-grained in the future. */\\n697:     premature = TRUE;\\n698:   default:\\n699:     break;\\n700:   }\\n701: \\n702:   /* this calls the protocol-specific function pointer previously set */\\n703:   if(conn->handler->done)\\n704:     result = conn->handler->done(data, status, premature);\\n705:   else\\n706:     result = status;\\n707: \\n708:   if(CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK != result) {\\n709:     /* avoid this if we already aborted by callback to avoid this calling\\n710:        another callback */\\n711:     int rc = Curl_pgrsDone(data);\\n712:     if(!result && rc)\\n713:       result = CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK;\\n714:   }\\n715: \\n716:   /* Inform connection filters that this transfer is done */\\n717:   Curl_conn_ev_data_done(data, premature);\\n718: \\n719:   process_pending_handles(data->multi); /* connection / multiplex */\\n720: \\n721:   Curl_safefree(data->state.ulbuf);\\n722: \\n723:   Curl_client_cleanup(data);\\n724: \\n725:   CONNCACHE_LOCK(data);\\n726:   Curl_detach_connection(data);\\n727:   if(CONN_INUSE(conn)) {\\n728:     /* Stop if still used. */\\n729:     CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\\n730:     DEBUGF(infof(data, \\\"Connection still in use %zu, \\\"\\n731:                  \\\"no more multi_done now!\\\",\\n732:                  conn->easyq.size));\\n733:     return CURLE_OK;\\n734:   }\\n735: \\n736:   data->state.done = TRUE; /* called just now! */\\n737: \\n738:   if(conn->dns_entry) {\\n739:     Curl_resolv_unlock(data, conn->dns_entry); /* done with this */\\n740:     conn->dns_entry = NULL;\\n741:   }\\n742:   Curl_hostcache_prune(data);\\n743: \\n744:   /* if data->set.reuse_forbid is TRUE, it means the libcurl client has\\n745:      forced us to close this connection. This is ignored for requests taking\\n746:      place in a NTLM/NEGOTIATE authentication handshake\\n747: \\n748:      if conn->bits.close is TRUE, it means that the connection should be\\n749:      closed in spite of all our efforts to be nice, due to protocol\\n750:      restrictions in our or the server's end\\n751: \\n752:      if premature is TRUE, it means this connection was said to be DONE before\\n753:      the entire request operation is complete and thus we can't know in what\\n754:      state it is for reusing, so we're forced to close it. In a perfect world\\n755:      we can add code that keep track of if we really must close it here or not,\\n756:      but currently we have no such detail knowledge.\\n757:   */\\n758: \\n759:   data->state.recent_conn_id = conn->connection_id;\\n760:   if((data->set.reuse_forbid\\n761: #if defined(USE_NTLM)\\n762:       && !(conn->http_ntlm_state == NTLMSTATE_TYPE2 ||\\n763:            conn->proxy_ntlm_state == NTLMSTATE_TYPE2)\\n764: #endif\\n765: #if defined(USE_SPNEGO)\\n766:       && !(conn->http_negotiate_state == GSS_AUTHRECV ||\\n767:            conn->proxy_negotiate_state == GSS_AUTHRECV)\\n768: #endif\\n769:      ) || conn->bits.close\\n770:        || (premature && !Curl_conn_is_multiplex(conn, FIRSTSOCKET))) {\\n771:     DEBUGF(infof(data, \\\"multi_done, not reusing connection=%\\\"\\n772:                        CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T \\\", forbid=%d\\\"\\n773:                        \\\", close=%d, premature=%d, conn_multiplex=%d\\\",\\n774:                  conn->connection_id,\\n775:                  data->set.reuse_forbid, conn->bits.close, premature,\\n776:                  Curl_conn_is_multiplex(conn, FIRSTSOCKET)));\\n777:     connclose(conn, \\\"disconnecting\\\");\\n778:     Curl_conncache_remove_conn(data, conn, FALSE);\\n779:     CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\\n780:     Curl_disconnect(data, conn, premature);\\n781:   }\\n782:   else {\\n783:     char buffer[256];\\n784:     const char *host =\\n785: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\\n786:       conn->bits.socksproxy ?\\n787:       conn->socks_proxy.host.dispname :\\n788:       conn->bits.httpproxy ? Curl_sh_entry *check;\\n299: \\n300:   if(there) {\\n301:     /* it is present, return fine */\\n302:     return there;\\n303:   }\\n304: \\n305:   /* not present, add it */\\n306:   check = calloc(1, sizeof(struct Curl_sh_entry));\\n307:   if(!check)\\n308:     return NULL; /* major failure */\\n309: \\n310:   Curl_hash_init(&check->transfers, TRHASH_SIZE, trhash, trhash_compare,\\n311:                  trhash_dtor);\\n312: \\n313:   /* make/add new hash entry */\\n314:   if(!Curl_hash_add(sh, (char *)&s, sizeof(curl_socket_t), check)) {\\n315:     Curl_hash_destroy(&check->transfers);\\n316:     free(check);\\n317:     return NULL; /* major failure */\\n318:   }\\n319: \\n320:   return check; /* things are good in sockhash land */\\n321: }\\n322: \\n323: \\n324: /* delete the given socket + Curl_hash_destroy(&entry->transfers);\\n329: \\n330:   /* We remove the hash entry. This will end up in a call to\\n331:      sh_freeentry(). */\\n332:   Curl_hash_delete(sh, (char *)&s, sizeof(curl_socket_t));\\n333: }\\n334: \\n335: /*\\n336:  * free a sockhash entry\\n337:  */\\n338: static void sh_freeentry(void *freethis)\\n339: {\\n340:   struct Curl_sh_entry *p = (struct Curl_sh_entry *) freethis;\\n341: \\n342:   free(p);\\n343: }\\n344: \\n345: static size_t fd_key_compare(void *k1, size_t k1_len, void *k2, size_t k2_len)\\n346: {\\n347:   (void) k1_len; (void) k2_len;\\n348: \\n349:   return (*((curl_socket_t *) k1)) == (*((curl_socket_t *) k2));\\n350: }\\n351: \\n352: static size_t hash_fd(void *key, size_t key_length, size_t slots_num)\\n353: {\\n354:   curl_socket_t fd = *((curl_socket_t *) key);\\n355:   (void) key_length;\\n356: \\n357:   return (fd % slots_num);\\n358: }\\n359: \\n360: /*\\n361:  * sh_init() creates a new socket hash and returns the handle for it.\\n362:  *\\n363:  * Quote from README.multi_socket:\\n364:  *\\n365:  * \\\"Some tests at 7000 and 9000 connections showed that the socket hash lookup\\n366:  * is somewhat of a bottle neck. Its current implementation may be a bit too\\n367:  * limiting. It simply has a fixed-size array, and on each entry in the array\\n368:  * it has a linked list with entries. So the hash only checks which list to\\n369:  * scan through. The code I had used so for used a list with merely 7 slots\\n370:  * (as that is what the DNS hash uses) but with 7000 connections that would\\n371:  * make an average of 1000 nodes in each list to run through. I upped that to\\n372:  * 97 slots (I believe a prime is suitable) and noticed a significant speed\\n373:  * increase.  I need to reconsider the hash implementation or use a rather\\n374:  * large default value like this. At 9000 connections I was still below 10us\\n375:  * per call.\\\"\\n376:  *\\n377:  */\\n378: static void sh_init(struct Curl_hash *hash, int hashsize)\\n379: {\\n380:   Curl_hash_init(hash, hashsize, hash_fd, fd_key_compare,\\n381:                  sh_freeentry);\\n382: }\\n383: \\n384: /*\\n385:  * multi_addmsg()\\n386:  *\\n387:  * Called when a transfer is completed. Adds the given msg pointer to\\n388:  * the list kept in the multi handle.\\n389:  */\\n390: static void multi_addmsg(struct Curl_multi *multi, struct Curl_message *msg)\\n391: {\\n392:   Curl_llist_insert_next(&multi->msglist, multi->msglist.tail, msg,\\n393:                          &msg->list);\\n394: }\\n395: \\n396: struct Curl_multi *Curl_multi_handle(int hashsize, /* socket hash                CURL_DNS_HASH_SIZE);\\n460: }\\n461: \\n462: #if defined(DEBUGBUILD) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS)\\n463: static void multi_warn_debug(struct Curl_multi *multi, struct Curl_easy *data)\\n464: {\\n465:   if(!multi->warned) {\\n466:     infof(data, \\\"!!! }\\n155: \\n156: /* always use this function to change state, to make debugging easier */\\n157: static void mstate(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLMstate state\\n158: #ifdef DEBUGBUILD\\n159:                    , int lineno\\n160: #endif\\n161: )\\n162: {\\n163:   CURLMstate oldstate = data->mstate;\\n164:   static const init_multistate_func finit[MSTATE_LAST] = {\\n165:     NULL,              /* INIT /*\\n222:  * We add one of these structs to the sockhash for each socket\\n223:  */\\n224: \\n225: struct Curl_sh_entry {\\n226:   struct Curl_hash transfers; /* hash of transfers using this socket \\n272: /*\\n273:  * The sockhash has its own separate subhash in each entry that need to be\\n274:  * safely destroyed first.\\n275:  */\\n276: static void sockhash_destroy(struct Curl_hash *h)\\n277: {\\n278:   struct Curl_hash_iterator iter;\\n279:   struct Curl_hash_element *he;\\n280: \\n281:   DEBUGASSERT(h);\\n282:   Curl_hash_start_iterate(h, &iter);\\n283:   he =       trhash_dtor);\\n312: \\n313:   /* make/add new hash entry */\\n314:   if(!Curl_hash_add(sh, (char *)&s, sizeof(curl_socket_t), check)) {\\n315:     Curl_hash_destroy(&check->transfers);\\n316:     free(check);\\n317:     return NULL; /* major failure */\\n318:   }\\n319: \\n320:   return check; /* things are good in sockhash land */\\n321: }\\n322: \\n323: \\n324: /* delete the given socket + handle from the hash */\\n325: static void sh_delentry(struct Curl_sh_entry *entry,\\n326:                         struct Curl_hash *sh, curl_socket_t s)\\n327: {\\n328: #endif\n220: \n221: /*\n222:  * We add one of these structs to the sockhash for each socket\n223:  */\n224: \n225: struct Curl_sh_entry {\n226:   struct Curl_hash transfers; /* hash of transfers using /*\n273:  * The sockhash has its own separate subhash in each entry that need to be\n274:  * safely destroyed first.\n275:  */\n276: static void sockhash_destroy(struct Curl_hash *h)\n277: {\n278:   struct Curl_hash_iterator iter;\n279:   struct Curl_hash_element *he;\n280: \n281:   DEBUGASSERT(h);\n282:   Curl_hash_start_iterate(h, &iter);\n283:   he = Curl_hash_next_element(&iter);\n284:   while(he) {\n285:     struct Curl_sh_entry *sh = (struct Curl_sh_entry *)he->ptr;\n286:     Curl_hash_destroy(&sh->transfers);\n287:     he = Curl_hash_next_element(&iter);\n288:   }\n289:   Curl_hash_destroy(h);\n290: }\n291: \n292: \n293: /* make sure this socket is present in the hash for this handle */\n294: static struct Curl_sh_entry *sh_addentry(struct Curl_hash *sh,\n295:                                          curl_socket_t s)\n296: {\n297:   struct Curl_sh_entry *there = sh_getentry(sh, s);\n298:   struct Curl_sh_entry *check;\n299: \n300:   if(there) {\n301:     /* it is present, return fine */\n302:     return there;\n303:   }\n304: \n305:   /* not present, add it */\n306:   check = calloc(1, sizeof(struct Curl_sh_entry));\n307:   if(!check)\n308:     return NULL; /* \n320:   return check; /* things are good in sockhash land */\n321: }\n322: \n323: \n324: /* delete the given socket + handle from the hash */\n325: static void sh_delentry(struct Curl_sh_entry *entry,\n326:                         struct Curl_hash *sh, curl_socket_t s)\n327: {\n328:   Curl_hash_destroy(&entry->transfers);\n329: \n330:   /* We remove the hash entry. Its current implementation may be a bit too\n367:  * limiting. It simply has a fixed-size array, and on each entry in the array\n368:  * it has a linked list with entries. So the hash only checks which list to\n369:  * scan through. The code I had used so for used a list with merely 7 slots\n370:  * (as that is what the DNS hash uses) but with 7000 connections that would\n371:  * make an average of 1000 nodes in each list to run through. I upped that to\n372:  * 97 slots (I believe a prime is suitable) and noticed a significant speed\n373:  * increase.  I need to reconsider the hash implementation or use a rather\n374:  * large default value like this. At 9000 connections I was still below 10us\n375:  * per call.\"\n376:  *\n377:  */\n378: static void sh_init(struct Curl_hash *hash, int hashsize)\n379: {\n380:   Curl_hash_init(hash, hashsize, hash_fd, fd_key_compare,\n381:                  sh_freeentry);\n382: }\n383: \n384: /*\n385:  * multi_addmsg()\n386:  *\n387:  * Called when a transfer is completed. In a perfect world\\n755:      we can add code that keep track of if we really must close it here or not,\\n756:      but currently we have no such detail knowledge.\\n757:   */\\n758: \\n759: \n852:   if(multi->in_callback)\n853:     return CURLM_RECURSIVE_API_CALL;\n854: \n855:   premature = (data->mstate < MSTATE_COMPLETED) ? defined(CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS)\\n185:   (void) lineno;\\n186: #endif\\n187: \\n188:   if(oldstate == state)\\n189:     /* don't bother when the new state is the same as the old state */\\n190:
