          (void *)data, lineno);\\n201:   }\\n202: #endif\\n203: \\n204:   if(state == MSTATE_COMPLETED) {\\n205:     /* changing to COMPLETED means there's one less easy handle 'alive' */\\n206:     DEBUGASSERT(data->multi->num_alive > 0);\\n207:     data->multi->num_alive--;\\n208:   }\\n209: \\n210:   /* if this state has an init-function, run it */\\n211:   if(finit[state])\\n212:     finit[state](data);\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: #ifndef DEBUGBUILD\\n216: #define multistate(x,y) mstate(x,y)\\n217: #else\\n218: #define multistate(x,y) mstate(x,y, __LINE__)\\n219: #endif\\n220: \\n221: /*\\n222:  * We add one of these structs to the sockhash for each socket\\n223:  */\\n224: \\n225: struct Curl_sh_entry {\\n226:   struct Curl_hash transfers; /* hash of transfers using this socket */\\n227:   unsigned int action;  /* what combined action READ/WRITE this socket waits\\n228:                            for */\\n229:   unsigned int users; /* number of transfers using this */\\n230:   void *socketp; /* settable by users with curl_multi_assign() */\\n231:   unsigned int readers; /* this many transfers want to read */\\n232:   unsigned int writers; /* this many transfers want to write */\\n233: };\\n234: /* bits for 'action' having no bits means this socket is not expecting any\\n235:    action */\\n236: #define SH_READ  1\\n237: #define SH_WRITE 2\\n238: \\n239: /* look up a given socket in the socket hash, skip invalid sockets */\\n240: static struct Curl_sh_entry *sh_getentry(struct Curl_hash *sh,\\n241:                                          curl_socket_t s)\\n242: {\\n243:   if(s != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) {\\n244:     /* only look for proper sockets */\\n245:     return Curl_hash_pick(sh, (char *)&s, sizeof(curl_socket_t));\\n246:   }\\n247:   return NULL;\\n248: }\\n249: \\n250: #define TRHASH_SIZE 13\\n251: static size_t trhash(void *key, size_t key_length, size_t slots_num)\\n252: {\\n253:   size_t keyval = (size_t)*(struct Curl_easy **)key;\\n254:   (void) key_length;\\n255: \\n256:   return (keyval % slots_num);\\n257: }\\n258: \\n259: static size_t trhash_compare(void *k1, size_t k1_len, void *k2, size_t k2_len)\\n260: {\\n261:   (void)k1_len;\\n262:   (void)k2_len;\\n263: \\n264:   return *(struct Curl_easy **)k1 == *(struct Curl_easy **)k2;\\n265: }\\n266: \\n267: static void trhash_dtor(void *nada)\\n268: {\\n269:   (void)nada;\\n270: }\\n271: \\n272: /*\\n273:  * The sockhash has its own separate subhash in each entry that need to be\\n274:  * safely destroyed first.\\n275:  */\\n276: static void sockhash_destroy(struct Curl_hash *h)\\n277: {\\n278:   struct Curl_hash_iterator iter;\\n279:   struct Curl_hash_element *he;\\n280: \\n281:   DEBUGASSERT(h);\\n282:   Curl_hash_start_iterate(h, &iter);\\n283:   he = Curl_hash_next_element(&iter);\\n284:   while(he) {\\n285:     struct Curl_sh_entry *sh = (struct Curl_sh_entry *)he->ptr;\\n286:     Curl_hash_destroy(&sh->transfers);\\n287:     he = Curl_hash_next_element(&iter);\\n288:   }\\n289:   Curl_hash_destroy(h);\\n290: }\\n291: \\n292: \\n293: /* make sure this socket is present in the hash for this handle */\\n294: static struct Curl_sh_entry *sh_addentry(struct Curl_hash *sh,\\n295:                                          curl_socket_t s)\\n296: {\\n297:   struct Curl_sh_entry *there = sh_getentry(sh, s);\\n298:   struct Curl_sh_entry *check;\\n299: \\n300:   if(there) {\\n301:     /* it is present, return fine */\\n302:     return there;\\n303:   }\\n304: \\n305:   /* not present, add it */\\n306:   check = calloc(1, sizeof(struct Curl_sh_entry));\\n307:   if(!check)\\n308:     return NULL; /* major failure */\\n309: \\n310:   Curl_hash_init(&check->transfers, TRHASH_SIZE, trhash, trhash_compare,\\n311:                  trhash_dtor);\\n312: \\n313:   /* make/add new hash entry */\\n314:   if(!Curl_hash_add(sh, (char *)&s, sizeof(curl_socket_t), check)) {\\n315:     Curl_hash_destroy(&check->transfers);\\n316:     free(check);\\n317:     return NULL; /* major failure */\\n318:   }\\n319: \\n320:   return check; /* things are good in sockhash land */\\n321: }\\n322: \\n323: \\n324: /* delete the given socket + handle from the hash */\\n325: static void sh_delentry(struct Curl_sh_entry *entry,\\n326:                         struct Curl_hash *sh, curl_socket_t s)\\n327: {\\n328:   Curl_hash_destroy(&entry->transfers);\\n329: \\n330:   /* We remove the hash entry. This will end up in a call to\\n331:      sh_freeentry(). */\\n332:   Curl_hash_delete(sh, (char *)&s, sizeof(curl_socket_t));\\n333: }\\n334: \\n335: /*\\n336:  * free a sockhash entry\\n337:  */\\n338: static void sh_freeentry(void *freethis)\\n339: {\\n340:   struct Curl_sh_entry *p = (struct Curl_sh_entry *) freethis;\\n341: \\n342:   free(p);\\n343: }\\n344: \\n345: static size_t fd_key_compare(void *k1, size_t k1_len, void *k2, size_t k2_len)\\n346: {\\n347:   (void) k1_len; (void) k2_len;\\n348: \\n349:   return (*((curl_socket_t *) k1)) == (*((curl_socket_t *) k2));\\n350: }\\n351: \\n352: static size_t hash_fd(void *key, size_t key_length, size_t slots_num)\\n353: {\\n354:   curl_socket_t fd = *((curl_socket_t *) key);\\n355:   (void) key_length;\\n356: \\n357:   return (fd % slots_num);\\n358: }\\n359: \\n360: /*\\n361:  * sh_init() creates a new socket hash and returns the handle for it.\\n362:  *\\n363:  * Quote from README.multi_socket:\\n364:  *\\n365:  * \\\"Some tests at 7000 and 9000 connections showed that the socket hash lookup\\n366:  * is somewhat of a bottle neck. Its current implementation may be a bit too\\n367:  * limiting. It simply has a fixed-size array, and on each entry in the array\\n368:  * it has a linked list with entries. So the hash only checks which list to\\n369:  * scan through. The code I had used so for used a list with merely 7 slots\\n370:  * (as that is what the DNS hash uses) but with 7000 connections that would\\n371:  * make an average of 1000 nodes in each list to run through. I upped that to\\n372:  * 97 slots (I believe a prime is suitable) and noticed a significant speed\\n373:  * increase.  I need to reconsider the hash implementation or use a rather\\n374:  * large default value like this. At 9000 connections I was still below 10us\\n375:  * per call.\\\"\\n376:  *\\n377:  */\\n378: static void sh_init(struct Curl_hash *hash, int hashsize)\\n379: {\\n380:   Curl_hash_init(hash, hashsize, hash_fd, fd_key_compare,\\n381:                  sh_freeentry);\\n382: }\\n383: \\n384: /*\\n385:  * multi_addmsg()\\n386:  *\\n387:  * Called when a transfer is completed. Adds the given msg pointer to\\n388:  * the list kept in the multi handle.\\n389:  */\\n390: static void multi_addmsg(struct Curl_multi *multi, struct Curl_message *msg)\\n391: {\\n392:   Curl_llist_insert_next(&multi->msglist, multi->msglist.tail, msg,\\n393:                          &msg->list);\\n394: }\\n395: \\n396: struct Curl_multi *Curl_multi_handle(int hashsize, /* socket hash */\\n397:                                      int chashsize, /* connection hash */\\n398:                                      int dnssize) /* dns hash */\\n399: {\\n400:   struct Curl_multi *multi = calloc(1, sizeof(struct Curl_multi));\\n401: \\n402:   if(!multi)\\n403:     return NULL;\\n404: \\n405:   multi->magic = CURL_MULTI_HANDLE;\\n406: \\n407:   Curl_init_dnscache(&multi->hostcache, dnssize);\\n408: \\n409:   sh_init(&multi->sockhash, hashsize);\\n410: \\n411:   if(Curl_conncache_init(&multi->conn_cache, chashsize))\\n412:     goto error;\\n413: \\n414:   Curl_llist_init(&multi->msglist, NULL);\\n415:   Curl_llist_init(&multi->pending, NULL);\\n416:   Curl_llist_init(&multi->msgsent, NULL);\\n417: \\n418:   multi->multiplexing = TRUE;\\n419: \\n420:   /* -1 means it not set by user, use the default value */\\n421:   multi->maxconnects = -1;\\n422:   multi->max_concurrent_streams = 100;\\n423: \\n424: #ifdef USE_WINSOCK\\n425:   multi->wsa_event = WSACreateEvent();\\n426:   if(multi->wsa_event == WSA_INVALID_EVENT)\\n427:     goto error;\\n428: #else\\n429: #ifdef ENABLE_WAKEUP\\n430:   if(wakeup_create(multi->wakeup_pair) < 0) {\\n431:     multi->wakeup_pair[0] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\\n432:     multi->wakeup_pair[1] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\\n433:   }\\n434:   else if(curlx_nonblock(multi->wakeup_pair[0], TRUE) < 0 ||\\n435:           curlx_nonblock(multi->wakeup_pair[1], TRUE) < 0) {\\n436:     wakeup_close(multi->wakeup_pair[0]);\\n437:     wakeup_close(multi->wakeup_pair[1]);\\n438:     multi->wakeup_pair[0] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\\n439:     multi->wakeup_pair[1] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\\n440:   }\\n441: #endif\\n442: #endif\\n443: \\n444:   return multi;\\n445: \\n446: error:\\n447: \\n448:   sockhash_destroy(&multi->sockhash);\\n449:   Curl_hash_destroy(&multi->hostcache);\\n450:   Curl_conncache_destroy(&multi->conn_cache);\\n451:   free(multi);\\n452:   return NULL;\\n453: }\\n454: \\n455: struct Curl_multi *curl_multi_init(void)\\n456: {\\n457:   return Curl_multi_handle(CURL_SOCKET_HASH_TABLE_SIZE,\\n458:                            CURL_CONNECTION_HASH_SIZE,\\n459:                            CURL_DNS_HASH_SIZE);\\n460: }\\n461: \\n462: #if defined(DEBUGBUILD) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS)\\n463: static void multi_warn_debug(struct Curl_multi *multi, struct Curl_easy *data)\\n464: {\\n465:   if(!multi->warned) {\\n466:     infof(data, \\\"!!! WARNING !!!\\\");\\n467:     infof(data, \\\"This is a debug build of libcurl, \\\"\\n468:           \\\"do not use in production.\\\");\\n469:     multi->warned = true;\\n470:   }\\n471: }\\n472: #else\\n473: #define multi_warn_debug(x,y) Curl_nop_stmt\\n474: #endif\\n475: \\n476: /* returns TRUE if the easy handle is supposed to be present in the main link\\n477:    list */\\n478: static bool in_main_list(struct Curl_easy *data)\\n479: {\\n480:   return ((data->mstate != MSTATE_PENDING) &&\\n481:           (data->mstate != MSTATE_MSGSENT));\\n482: }\\n483: \\n484: static void link_easy(struct Curl_multi *multi,\\n485:                       struct Curl_easy *data)\\n486: {\\n487:   /* We add the new easy entry last in the list. */\\n488:   data->next = NULL; /* end of the line */\\n489:   if(multi->easyp) {\\n490:     struct Curl_easy *last = multi->easylp;\\n491:     last->next = data;\\n492:     data->prev = last;\\n493:     multi->easylp = data; /* the new last node */\\n494:   }\\n495:   else {\\n496:     /* first node, make prev NULL! */\\n497:     data->prev = NULL;\\n498:     multi->easylp = multi->easyp = data; /* both first and last */\\n499:   }\\n500: }\\n501: \\n502: /* unlink the given easy handle from the linked list of easy handles */\\n503: static void unlink_easy(struct Curl_multi *multi,\\n504:                         struct Curl_easy *data)\\n505: {\\n506:   /* make the previous node point to our next */\\n507:   if(data->prev)\\n508:     data->prev->next = data->next;\\n509:   else\\n510:     multi->easyp = data->next; /* point to first node */\\n511: \\n512:   /* make our next point to our previous node */\\n513:   if(data->next)\\n514:     data->next->prev = data->prev;\\n515:   else\\n516:     multi->easylp = data->prev; /* point to last node */\\n517: \\n518:   data->prev = data->next = NULL;\\n519: }\\n520: \\n521: \\n522: CURLMcode curl_multi_add_handle(struct Curl_multi *multi,\\n523:                                 struct Curl_easy *data)\\n524: {\\n525:   CURLMcode rc;\\n526:   /* First, make some basic checks that the CURLM handle is a good handle */\\n527:   if(!GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE(multi))\\n528:     return CURLM_BAD_HANDLE;\\n529: \\n530:   /* Verify that we got a somewhat good easy handle too */\\n531:   if(!GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(data))\\n532:     return CURLM_BAD_EASY_HANDLE;\\n533: \\n534:   /* Prevent users from adding same easy handle more than once and prevent\\n535:      adding to more than one multi stack */\\n536:   if(data->multi)\\n537:     return CURLM_ADDED_ALREADY;\\n538: \\n539:   if(multi->in_callback)\\n540:     return CURLM_RECURSIVE_API_CALL;\\n541: \\n542:   if(multi->dead) {\\n543:     /* a \\\"dead\\\" handle cannot get added transfers while any existing easy\\n544:        handles are still alive - but if there are none alive anymore, it is\\n545:        fine to start over and unmark the \\\"deadness\\\" of this handle */\\n546:     if(multi->num_alive)\\n547:       return CURLM_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK;\\n548:     multi->dead = FALSE;\\n549:   }\\n550: \\n551:   /* Initialize timeout list for this handle */\\n552:   Curl_llist_init(&data->state.timeoutlist, NULL);\\n553: \\n554:   /*\\n555:    * No failure allowed in this function beyond this point. And no\\n556:    * modification of easy nor multi handle allowed before this except for\\n557:    * potential multi's connection cache growing which won't be undone in this\\n558:    * function no matter what.\\n559:    */\\n560:   if(data->set.errorbuffer)\\n561:     data->set.errorbuffer[0] = 0;\\n562: \\n563:   /* make the Curl_easy refer back to this multi handle - before Curl_expire()\\n564:      is called. */\\n565:   data->multi = multi;\\n566: \\n567:   /* Set the timeout for this handle to expire really soon so that it will\\n568:      be taken care of even when this handle is added in the midst of operation\\n569:      when only the curl_multi_socket() API is used. During that flow, only\\n570:      sockets that time-out or have actions will be dealt with. Since this\\n571:      handle has no action yet, we make sure it times out to get things to\\n572:      happen. */\\n573:   Curl_expire(data, 0, EXPIRE_RUN_NOW);\\n574: \\n575:   /* A somewhat crude work-around for a little glitch in Curl_update_timer()\\n576:      that happens if the lastcall time is set to the same time when the handle\\n577:      is removed as when the next handle is added, as then the check in\\n578:      Curl_update_timer() that prevents calling the application multiple times\\n579:      with the same timer info will not trigger and then the new handle's\\n580:      timeout will not be notified to the app.\\n581: \\n582:      The work-around is thus simply to clear the 'lastcall' variable to force\\n583:      Curl_update_timer() to always trigger a callback to the app when a new\\n584:      easy handle is added */\\n585:   memset(&multi->timer_lastcall, 0, sizeof(multi->timer_lastcall));\\n586: \\n587:   rc = Curl_update_timer(multi);\\n588:   if(rc)\\n589:     return rc;\\n590: \\n591:   /* set the easy handle */\\n592:   multistate(data, MSTATE_INIT);\\n593: \\n594:   /* for multi interface connections, we share DNS cache automatically if the\\n595:      easy handle's one is currently not set. */\\n596:   if(!data->dns.hostcache ||\\n597:      (data->dns.hostcachetype == HCACHE_NONE)) {\\n598:     data->dns.hostcache = &multi->hostcache;\\n599:     data->dns.hostcachetype = HCACHE_MULTI;\\n600:   }\\n601: \\n602:   /* Point to the shared or multi handle connection cache */\\n603:   if(data->share && (data->share->specifier & (1<< CURL_LOCK_DATA_CONNECT)))\\n604:     data->state.conn_cache = &data->share->conn_cache;\\n605:   else\\n606:     data->state.conn_cache = &multi->conn_cache;\\n607:   data->state.lastconnect_id = -1;\\n608: \\n609: #ifdef USE_LIBPSL\\n610:   /* Do the same for PSL. */\\n611:   if(data->share && (data->share->specifier & (1 << CURL_LOCK_DATA_PSL)))\\n612:     data->psl = &data->share->psl;\\n613:   else\\n614:     data->psl = &multi->psl;\\n615: #endif\\n616: \\n617:   link_easy(multi, data);\\n618: \\n619:   /* increase the node-counter */\\n620:   multi->num_easy++;\\n621: \\n622:   /* increase the alive-counter */\\n623:   multi->num_alive++;\\n624: \\n625:   CONNCACHE_LOCK(data);\\n626:   /* The closure handle only ever has default timeouts set. To improve the\\n627:      state somewhat we clone the timeouts from each added handle so that the\\n628:      closure handle always has the same timeouts as the most recently added\\n629:      easy handle. */\\n630:   data->state.conn_cache->closure_handle->set.timeout = data->set.timeout;\\n631:   data->state.conn_cache->closure_handle->set.server_response_timeout =\\n632:     data->set.server_response_timeout;\\n633:   data->state.conn_cache->closure_handle->set.no_signal =\\n634:     data->set.no_signal;\\n635:   data->id = data->state.conn_cache->next_easy_id++;\\n636:   if(data->state.conn_cache->next_easy_id <= 0)\\n637:     data->state.conn_cache->next_easy_id = 0;\\n638:   CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\\n639: \\n640:   multi_warn_debug(multi, data);\\n641: \\n642:   return CURLM_OK;\\n643: }\\n644: \\n645: #if 0\\n646: /* Debug-function, used like this:\\n647:  *\\n648:  * Curl_hash_print(&multi->sockhash, debug_print_sock_hash);\\n649:  *\\n650:  * Enable the hash print function first by editing hash.c\\n651:  */\\n652: static void debug_print_sock_hash(void *p)\\n653: {\\n654:   struct Curl_sh_entry *sh = (struct Curl_sh_entry *)p;\\n655: \\n656:   fprintf(stderr, \\\" [readers %u][writers %u]\\\",\\n657:           sh->readers, sh->writers);\\n658: }\\n659: #endif\\n660: \\n661: static CURLcode multi_done(struct Curl_easy *data,\\n662:                            CURLcode status,  /* an error if this is called\\n663:                                                 after an error was detected */\\n664:                            bool premature)\\n665: {\\n666:   CURLcode result;\\n667:   struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\\n668: \\n669: #if defined(DEBUGBUILD) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS)\\n670:   DEBUGF(infof(data, \\\"multi_done[%s]: status: %d prem: %d done: %d\\\",\\n671:                multi_statename[data->mstate],\\n672:                (int)status, (int)premature, data->state.done));\\n673: #else\\n674:   DEBUGF(infof(data, \\\"multi_done: status: %d prem: %d done: %d\\\",\\n675:                (int)status, (int)premature, data->state.done));\\n676: #endif\\n677: \\n678:   if(data->state.done)\\n679:     /* Stop if multi_done() has already been called */\\n680:     return CURLE_OK;\\n681: \\n682:   /* Stop the resolver and free its own resources (but not dns_entry yet). */\\n683:   Curl_resolver_kill(data);\\n684: \\n685:   /* Cleanup possible redirect junk */\\n686:   Curl_safefree(data->req.newurl);\\n687:   Curl_safefree(data->req.location);\\n688: \\n689:   switch(status) {\\n690:   case CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK:\\n691:   case CURLE_READ_ERROR:\\n692:   case CURLE_WRITE_ERROR:\\n693:     /* When we're aborted due to a callback return code it basically have to\\n694:        be counted as premature as there is trouble ahead if we don't. We have\\n695:        many callbacks and protocols work differently, we could potentially do\\n696:        this more fine-grained in the future. */\\n697:     premature = TRUE;\\n698:   default:\\n699:     break;\\n700:   }\\n701: \\n702:   /* this calls the protocol-specific function pointer previously set */\\n703:   if(conn->handler->done)\\n704:     result = conn->handler->done(data, status, premature);\\n705:   else\\n706:     result = status;\\n707: \\n708:   if(CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK != result) {\\n709:     /* avoid this if we already aborted by callback to avoid this calling\\n710:        another callback */\\n711:     int rc = Curl_pgrsDone(data);\\n712:     if(!result && rc)\\n713:       result = CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK;\\n714:   }\\n715: \\n716:   /* Inform connection filters that this transfer is done */\\n717:   Curl_conn_ev_data_done(data, premature);\\n718: \\n719:   process_pending_handles(data->multi); /* connection / multiplex */\\n720: \\n721:   Curl_safefree(data->state.ulbuf);\\n722: \\n723:   Curl_client_cleanup(data);\\n724: \\n725:   CONNCACHE_LOCK(data);\\n726:   Curl_detach_connection(data);\\n727:   if(CONN_INUSE(conn)) {\\n728:     /* Stop if still used. */\\n729:     CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\\n730:     DEBUGF(infof(data, \\\"Connection still in use %zu, \\\"\\n731:                  \\\"no more multi_done now!\\\",\\n732:                  conn->easyq.size));\\n733:     return CURLE_OK;\\n734:   }\\n735: \\n736:   data->state.done = TRUE; /* called just now! */\\n737: \\n738:   if(conn->dns_entry) {\\n739:     Curl_resolv_unlock(data, conn->dns_entry); /* done with this */\\n740:     conn->dns_entry = NULL;\\n741:   }\\n742:   Curl_hostcache_prune(data);\\n743: \\n744:   /* if data->set.reuse_forbid is TRUE, it means the libcurl client has\\n745:      forced us to close this connection. This is ignored for requests taking\\n746:      place in a NTLM/NEGOTIATE authentication handshake\\n747: \\n748:      if conn->bits.close is TRUE, it means that the connection should be\\n749:      closed in spite of all our efforts to be nice, due to protocol\\n750:      restrictions in our or the server's end\\n751: \\n752:      if premature is TRUE, it means this connection was said to be DONE before\\n753:      the entire request operation is complete and thus we can't know in what\\n754:      state it is for reusing, so we're forced to close it. In a perfect world\\n755:      we can add code that keep track of if we really must close it here or not,\\n756:      but currently we have no such detail knowledge.\\n757:   */\\n758: \\n759:   data->state.recent_conn_id = conn->connection_id;\\n760:   if((data->set.reuse_forbid\\n761: #if defined(USE_NTLM)\\n762:       && !(conn->http_ntlm_state == NTLMSTATE_TYPE2 ||\\n763:            conn->proxy_ntlm_state == NTLMSTATE_TYPE2)\\n764: #endif\\n765: #if defined(USE_SPNEGO)\\n766:       && !(conn->http_negotiate_state == GSS_AUTHRECV ||\\n767:            conn->proxy_negotiate_state == GSS_AUTHRECV)\\n768: #endif\\n769:      ) || conn->bits.close\\n770:        || (premature && !Curl_conn_is_multiplex(conn, FIRSTSOCKET))) {\\n771:     DEBUGF(infof(data, \\\"multi_done, not reusing connection=%\\\"\\n772:                        CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T \\\", forbid=%d\\\"\\n773:                        \\\", close=%d, premature=%d, conn_multiplex=%d\\\",\\n774:                  conn->connection_id,\\n775:                  data->set.reuse_forbid, conn->bits.close, premature,\\n776:                  Curl_conn_is_multiplex(conn, FIRSTSOCKET)));\\n777:     connclose(conn, \\\"disconnecting\\\");\\n778:     Curl_conncache_remove_conn(data, conn, FALSE);\\n779:     CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\\n780:     Curl_disconnect(data, conn, premature);\\n781:   }\\n782:   else {\\n783:     char buffer[256];\\n784:     const char *host =\\n785: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\\n786:       conn->bits.socksproxy ?\\n787:       conn->socks_proxy.host.dispname :\\n788:       conn->bits.httpproxy ? conn->http_proxy.host.dispname :\\n789: #endif\\n790:       conn->bits.conn_to_host ? conn->conn_to_host.dispname :\\n791:       conn->host.dispname;\\n792:     /* create string before returning the connection */\\n793:     curl_off_t connection_id = conn->connection_id;\\n794:     msnprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer),\\n795:               \\\"Connection #%\\\" pre-check (pollrc == 0) */\\n1343:       WSAWaitForMultipleEvents(1, &multi->wsa_event, FALSE, timeout_ms, FALSE);\\n1344:     }\\n1345:     /* With WinSock, we have to run the following section unconditionally\\n1346:        to call WSAEventSelect(fd, event, 0) on all the sockets */\\n1347:     {\\n1348: #endif\\n1349:       /* copy revents results from the poll to the curl_multi_wait poll\\n1350:          struct, the bit values of the actual underlying poll() implementation\\n1351:          may not be the same as the ones in the public libcurl API! */\\n1352:       for(i = 0; i < extra_nfds; i++) {\\n1353:         unsigned r = ufds[curlfds + i].revents;\\n1354:         unsigned short mask = 0;\\n1355: #ifdef USE_WINSOCK\\n1356:         curl_socket_t s = extra_fds[i].fd;\\n1357:         wsa_events.lNetworkEvents = 0;\\n1358:         if(WSAEnumNetworkEvents(s, NULL, &wsa_events) == 0) {\\n1359:           if(wsa_events.lNetworkEvents & (FD_READ|FD_ACCEPT|FD_CLOSE))\\n1360:             mask |= CURL_WAIT_POLLIN;\\n1361:           if(wsa_events.lNetworkEvents & (FD_WRITE|FD_CONNECT|FD_CLOSE))\\n1362:             mask |= continue;\\n1372:         }\\n1373: #endif\\n1374:         if(r & POLLIN)\\n1375:           mask |= CURL_WAIT_POLLIN;\\n1376:         if(r & POLLOUT)\\n1377:           mask |= CURL_WAIT_POLLOUT;\\n1378:         if(r & POLLPRI)\\n1379:           mask |= CURL_WAIT_POLLPRI;\\n1380:         extra_fds[i].revents = mask;\\n1381:       }\\n1382: \\n1383: #ifdef USE_WINSOCK\\n1384:       /* Count up all our own sockets that had activity,\\n1385:          and remove them from the event. */\\n1386:       if(curlfds) {\\n1387: \\n1388:         for(data = multi->easyp; data; data = data->next) {\\n1389:           bitmap = multi_getsock(data, sockbunch);\\n1390: \\n1391:           for(i = 0; i < MAX_SOCKSPEREASYHANDLE; i++) {\\n1392:             if(bitmap & (GETSOCK_READSOCK(i) |         if(ufds[curlfds + extra_nfds].revents & POLLIN) {\\n1413:           char buf[64];\\n1414:           ssize_t nread;\\n1415:           while(1) {\\n1416:             /* the reading socket is non-blocking, try to read\\n1417:                data from it until it receives an error (except EINTR).\\n1418:                In normal cases it will get EAGAIN or EWOULDBLOCK\\n1419:                when there is no more data, breaking the loop. */\\n1420:             nread = wakeup_read(multi->wakeup_pair[0], buf, sizeof(buf));\\n1421:             if(nread <= 0) {\\n1422:               if(nread < 0 && EINTR == SOCKERRNO)\\n1423:                 continue;\\n1424:               break;\\n1425:             }\\n1426:           }\\n1427:           /* do not count the wakeup socket into the returned value */\\n1428:           retcode--;\\n1429:         }\\n1430:       }\\n1431: #endif\\n1432: #endif\\n1433:     }\\n1434:   }\\n1435: \\n1436:   if(ufds_malloc)\\n1437: this holds a -1\\n1452:          timeout */\\n1453:       else if(sleep_ms < 0)\\n1454:         sleep_ms = timeout_ms;\\n1455:       Curl_wait_ms(sleep_ms);\\n1456:     }\\n1457:   }\\n1458: \\n1459: extra_nfds, timeout_ms, ret, TRUE,\\n1479:                     TRUE);\\n1480: }\\n1481: \\n1482: CURLMcode curl_multi_wakeup(struct Curl_multi *multi)\\n1483: {\\n1484:   /* this function is usually called from another thread,\\n1485:      it has to be careful only to access parts of the\\n1486:      Curl_multi struct that are constant */\\n1487: \\n1488:   /* GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE can be safely another thread after the init part\\n1499:      and before cleanup */\\n1500:   if(multi->wakeup_pair[1] != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) {\\n1501:     char buf[1];\\n1502:     buf[0] = 1;\\n1503:     while(1) does not matter, which makes it ok to call.\\n1507: \\n1508:          The write socket is set to non-blocking, this way this function\\n1509:          cannot block, making it safe to call even from the same thread\\n1510:          that will call curl_multi_wait(). If swrite() returns that it\\n1511:          would block, it's considered successful because it means that\\n1512:          previous calls to this function will wake up the poll(). */\\n1513: CURLM_WAKEUP_FAILURE;\\n1532: }\\n1533: \\n1534: /*\\n1535:  * multi_ischanged() is called\\n1536:  *\\n1537:  * Returns TRUE/FALSE whether the state is changed to trigger a CONNECT_PEND\\n1538:  * => {\\n1544:   bool retval = multi->recheckstate;\\n1545:   if(clear)\\n1546:     multi->recheckstate = FALSE;\\n1547:   return retval;\\n1548: }\\n1549: \\n1550: /*\\n1551:  * Curl_multi_connchanged()               CURLcode *result,\\n1630:                                  bool connect_timeout)\\n1631: {\\n1632:   timediff_t timeout_ms;\\n1633:   timeout_ms = Curl_timeleft(data, now, \\n3483:   /* When multi_timeout() is done, multi->timetree points to the node with the\\n3484:    * timeout we got the (relative) time-out time for. Keep the list sorted so that head\\n3526:  * of list is always the timeout nearest in time.\\n3527:  *\\n3528:  */\\n3529: static CURLMcode\\n3530: multi_addtimeout(struct Curl_easy *data,\\n3531:                  struct curltime *stamp,\\n3532:                  expire_id eid)\\n3533: {\\n3534:   struct Curl_llist_element *e;\\n3535:   struct time_node *node;\\n3536:   struct Curl_llist_element *prev = NULL;\\n3537:   size_t n;\\n3538:   struct Curl_llist *timeoutlist = &data->state.timeoutlist;\\n3539: \\n3540:   node = &data->state.expires[eid];\\n3541: \\n3542:   /* copy the timestamp and id */\\n3543:   memcpy(&node->time, stamp, sizeof(*stamp));\\n3544:   node->eid = eid; /* also marks it as in use */\\n3545: \\n3546:   n = Curl_llist_count(timeoutlist);\\n3547:   if(n) {\\n3548:     /* find the correct spot in the list */\\n3549:     for(e = timeoutlist->head; e; e = e->next) {\\n3550:       struct time_node *check = (struct time_node *)e->ptr;\\n3551:       timediff_t diff = Curl_timediff(check->time, node->time);\\n3552:       if(diff > 0)\\n3553:         break;\\n3554:       prev = e;\\n3555:     }\\n3556: \\n3557:   }\\n3558:   /* else\\n3559:      this is the first timeout on the list */\\n3560: \\n3561:   Curl_llist_insert_next(timeoutlist, prev, node, &node->list);\\n3562:   return CURLM_OK;\\n3563: }\\n3564: \\n3565: /*\\n3566:  * Curl_expire()\\n3567:  *\\n3568:  * given a number of milliseconds from now to use to set the 'act before\\n3569:  * this'-time for the transfer, to be extracted by curl_multi_timeout()\\n3570:  *\\n3571:  * The timeout will be added to a queue of timeouts if it defines a moment in\\n3572:  * time that is later than the current head of queue.\\n3573:  *\\n3574:  * Expire replaces a former timeout using the same id if already set.\\n3575:  */\\n3576: void Curl_expire(struct Curl_easy *data, timediff_t milli, expire_id id)\\n3577: {\\n3578:   struct Curl_multi *multi = data->multi;\\n3579:   struct curltime *nowp = &data->state.expiretime;\\n3580:   struct curltime set;\\n3581: \\n3582:   /* this is only interesting while there is still an associated multi struct\\n3583:      remaining! */\\n3584:   if(!multi)\\n3585:     return;\\n3586: \\n3587:   DEBUGASSERT(id < EXPIRE_LAST);\\n3588: \\n3589:   set = Curl_now();\\n3590:   set.tv_sec += (time_t)(milli/1000); /* might be a 64 to 32 bit conversion */\\n3591:   set.tv_usec += (unsigned int)(milli%1000)*1000;\\n3592: \\n3593:   if(set.tv_usec >= 1000000) {\\n3594:     set.tv_sec++;\\n3595:     set.tv_usec -= 1000000;\\n3596:   }\\n3597: \\n3598:   /* Remove any timer with the same id just in case. */\\n3599:   multi_deltimeout(data, id);\\n3600: \\n3601:   /* Add it to the timer list. Still, every\n  CURL handle takes 45-50 K memory, therefore this 3K are not significant.\n*/\n#ifndef CURL_SOCKET_HASH_TABLE_SIZE\n#define CURL_SOCKET_HASH_TABLE_SIZE 911\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_CONNECTION_HASH_SIZE\n#define CURL_CONNECTION_HASH_SIZE 97\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DNS_HASH_SIZE\n#define CURL_DNS_HASH_SIZE 71\n#endif\n\n#define CURL_MULTI_HANDLE 0x000bab1e\n\n#ifdef DEBUGBUILD\n/* On a debug build, we want to fail hard on multi handles that\n * are not NULL, but no longer have the MAGIC touch. *multi);\n\n#ifdef DEBUGBUILD\nstatic const char * const multi_statename[]={\"INIT\",\n  \"PENDING\",\n  \"CONNECT\",\n  \"RESOLVING\",\n  \"CONNECTING\",\n  \"TUNNELING\",\n  \"PROTOCONNECT\",\n  \"PROTOCONNECTING\",\n  \"DO\",\n  \"DOING\",\n  \"DOING_MORE\",\n  \"DID\",\n  \"PERFORMING\",\n  \"RATELIMITING\",\n  \"DONE\",\n  \"COMPLETED\",\n  \"MSGSENT\",\n};\n#endif\n\n/* function pointer called once when switching TO a state */\ntypedef void (*init_multistate_func)(struct Curl_easy *data);\n\n/* called in DID state, before PERFORMING state */\nstatic void before_perform(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  data->req.chunk = FALSE;\n  Curl_pgrsTime(data, TIMER_PRETRANSFER);\n}\n\nstatic void init_completed(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  /* this is a completed transfer */\n\n  /* Important: reset the conn pointer so that we don't point to memory\n     that could be freed anytime */\n  Curl_detach_connection(data);\n  Curl_expire_clear(data); /* stop all timers */\n}\n\n/* always use this function to change state, to make debugging easier */\nstatic void mstate(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLMstate state\n#ifdef DEBUGBUILD\n                   , int lineno\n#endif\n)\n{\n  CURLMstate oldstate = data->mstate;\n  static const init_multistate_func finit[MSTATE_LAST] = {\n    NULL,              /* INIT */\n    NULL,              /* PENDING */\n    Curl_init_CONNECT, /* PERFORMING */\n    NULL,              /* RATELIMITING */\n    NULL,              /* DONE */\n    init_completed,    /* COMPLETED */\n    NULL               /* MSGSENT */\n  };\n\n#if return;\n\n  data->mstate = state;\n\n#if defined(DEBUGBUILD) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS)\n  if(data->mstate >= MSTATE_PENDING &&\n     data->mstate < MSTATE_COMPLETED) {\n MSTATE_COMPLETED) {\n    /* changing to COMPLETED means there's one less easy handle 'alive' */\n    DEBUGASSERT(data->multi->num_alive > 0);\n    data->multi->num_alive--;\n  }\n\n  /* if this state has an init-function, run it */\n  if(finit[state])\n    finit[state](data);\n}\n\n#ifndef DEBUGBUILD\n#define multistate(x,y) mstate(x,y)\n#else\n#define multistate(x,y) mstate(x,y, __LINE__)\n#endif\n\n/*\n * We add one of these structs to the sockhash for each socket\n */\n\nstruct Curl_sh_entry {\n  struct Curl_hash transfers; /* hash of transfers using this socket */\n  unsigned int action;  /* what combined action READ/WRITE this socket waits\n                           for */\n  unsigned int users; /* number of transfers using this */\n  void *socketp; /* settable by users with curl_multi_assign() */\n  unsigned int readers; /* this many transfers want to read */\n  unsigned int writers; /* this many transfers want to write */\n};\n/* bits for 'action' having no bits means this socket is not expecting any\n   action */\n#define SH_READ  1\n#define SH_WRITE 2\n\n/* look up a given socket in the socket hash, skip invalid sockets */\nstatic struct Curl_sh_entry *sh_getentry(struct Curl_hash *sh,\n                                         curl_socket_t s)\n{\n  if(s != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) {\n    /* only look for proper sockets */\n    return Curl_hash_pick(sh, (char *)&s, sizeof(curl_socket_t));\n  }\n  return NULL;\n}\n\n#define TRHASH_SIZE 13\nstatic size_t trhash(void *key, size_t key_length, size_t slots_num)\n{\n  size_t keyval = (size_t)*(struct Curl_easy **)key;\n  (void) key_length;\n\n  return (keyval % slots_num);\n}\n\nstatic size_t trhash_compare(void *k1, size_t k1_len, void *k2, size_t k2_len)\n{\n  (void)k1_len;\n  (void)k2_len;\n\n  return *(struct Curl_easy **)k1 == *(struct Curl_easy **)k2;\n}\n\nstatic void trhash_dtor(void *nada)\n{\n  (void)nada;\n}\n\n/*\n * The sockhash has its own separate subhash in each entry that need to be\n * safely destroyed first.\n */\nstatic void sockhash_destroy(struct Curl_hash *h)\n{\n  struct Curl_hash_iterator iter;\n  struct Curl_hash_element *he;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(h);\n  Curl_hash_start_iterate(h, &iter);\n  he = Curl_hash_next_element(&iter);\n  while(he) {\n    struct Curl_sh_entry *sh = (struct Curl_sh_entry *)he->ptr;\n    Curl_hash_destroy(&sh->transfers);\n    he = Curl_hash_next_element(&iter);\n  }\n  Curl_hash_destroy(h);\n}\n\n\n/* make sure this socket is present in the hash for this handle */\nstatic struct Curl_sh_entry *sh_addentry(struct Curl_hash *sh,\n                                         curl_socket_t s)\n{\n  struct Curl_sh_entry *there = sh_getentry(sh, s);\n  struct Curl_sh_entry *check;\n\n  if(there) {\n    /* it is present, return fine */\n    return there;\n  }\n\n  /* not present, add it */\n  check = calloc(1, sizeof(struct Curl_sh_entry));\n  if(!check)\n    return NULL; /* major failure */\n\n  Curl_hash_init(&check->transfers, TRHASH_SIZE, trhash, trhash_compare,\n                 trhash_dtor);\n\n  /* make/add new hash entry */\n  if(!Curl_hash_add(sh, (char *)&s, sizeof(curl_socket_t), check)) {\n    Curl_hash_destroy(&check->transfers);\n    free(check);\n    return NULL; /* major failure */\n  }\n\n  return check; /* things are good in sockhash land */\n}\n\n\n/* delete the given socket + handle from the hash */\nstatic void sh_delentry(struct Curl_sh_entry *entry,\n                        struct Curl_hash *sh, curl_socket_t s)\n{\n  Curl_hash_destroy(&entry->transfers);\n\n  /* We remove the hash entry. This will end up in a call to\n     sh_freeentry(). */\n  Curl_hash_delete(sh, (char *)&s, sizeof(curl_socket_t));\n}\n\n/*\n * free a sockhash entry\n */\nstatic void sh_freeentry(void *freethis)\n{\n  struct Curl_sh_entry *p = (struct Curl_sh_entry *) freethis;\n\n  free(p);\n}\n\nstatic size_t fd_key_compare(void *k1, size_t k1_len, void *k2, size_t k2_len)\n{\n  (void) k1_len; (void) k2_len;\n\n  return (*((curl_socket_t *) k1)) == (*((curl_socket_t *) k2));\n}\n\nstatic size_t hash_fd(void *key, size_t key_length, size_t slots_num)\n{\n  curl_socket_t fd = *((curl_socket_t *) key);\n  (void) key_length;\n\n  return (fd % slots_num);\n}\n\n/*\n * sh_init() creates a new socket hash and returns the handle for it.\n *\n * Quote from README.multi_socket:\n *\n * \"Some tests at 7000 and 9000 connections showed that the socket hash lookup\n * is somewhat of a bottle neck. Its current implementation may be a bit too\n * limiting. It simply has a fixed-size array, and on each entry in the array\n * it has a linked list with entries. So the hash only checks which list to\n * scan through. The code I had used so for used a list with merely 7 slots\n * (as that is what the DNS hash uses) but with 7000 connections that would\n * make an average of 1000 nodes in each list to run through. I upped that to\n * 97 slots (I believe a prime is suitable) and noticed a significant speed\n * increase.  I need to reconsider the hash implementation or use a rather\n * large default value like this. At 9000 connections I was still below 10us\n * per call.\"\n *\n */\nstatic void sh_init(struct Curl_hash *hash, int hashsize)\n{\n  Curl_hash_init(hash, hashsize, hash_fd, fd_key_compare,\n                 sh_freeentry);\n}\n\n/*\n * multi_addmsg()\n *\n * Called when a transfer is completed. Adds the given msg pointer to\n * the list kept in the multi handle.\n */\nstatic void multi_addmsg(struct Curl_multi *multi, struct Curl_message *msg)\n{\n  Curl_llist_insert_next(&multi->msglist, multi->msglist.tail, msg,\n                         &msg->list);\n}\n\nstruct Curl_multi *Curl_multi_handle(int hashsize, /* socket hash */\n                                     int chashsize, /* connection hash */\n                                     int dnssize) /* dns hash */\n{\n  struct Curl_multi *multi = calloc(1, sizeof(struct Curl_multi));\n\n  if(!multi)\n    return NULL;\n\n  multi->magic = CURL_MULTI_HANDLE;\n\n  Curl_init_dnscache(&multi->hostcache, dnssize);\n\n  sh_init(&multi->sockhash, hashsize);\n\n  if(Curl_conncache_init(&multi->conn_cache, chashsize))\n    goto error;\n\n  Curl_llist_init(&multi->msglist, NULL);\n  Curl_llist_init(&multi->pending, NULL);\n  Curl_llist_init(&multi->msgsent, NULL);\n\n  multi->multiplexing = TRUE;\n\n  /* -1 means it not set by user, use the default value */\n  multi->maxconnects = -1;\n  multi->max_concurrent_streams = 100;\n\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n  multi->wsa_event = WSACreateEvent();\n  if(multi->wsa_event == WSA_INVALID_EVENT)\n    goto error;\n#else\n#ifdef ENABLE_WAKEUP\n  if(wakeup_create(multi->wakeup_pair) < 0) {\n    multi->wakeup_pair[0] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\n    multi->wakeup_pair[1] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\n  }\n  else if(curlx_nonblock(multi->wakeup_pair[0], TRUE) < 0 ||\n          curlx_nonblock(multi->wakeup_pair[1], TRUE) < 0) {\n    wakeup_close(multi->wakeup_pair[0]);\n    wakeup_close(multi->wakeup_pair[1]);\n    multi->wakeup_pair[0] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\n    multi->wakeup_pair[1] = CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\n  }\n#endif\n#endif\n\n  return multi;\n\nerror:\n\n  sockhash_destroy(&multi->sockhash);\n  Curl_hash_destroy(&multi->hostcache);\n  Curl_conncache_destroy(&multi->conn_cache);\n  free(multi);\n  return NULL;\n}\n\nstruct Curl_multi *curl_multi_init(void)\n{\n  return Curl_multi_handle(CURL_SOCKET_HASH_TABLE_SIZE,\n                           CURL_CONNECTION_HASH_SIZE,\n                           CURL_DNS_HASH_SIZE);\n}\n\n#if defined(DEBUGBUILD) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS)\nstatic void multi_warn_debug(struct Curl_multi *multi, struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  if(!multi->warned) {\n    infof(data, \"!!! WARNING !!!\");\n    infof(data, \"This is a debug build of libcurl, \"\n          \"do not use in production.\");\n    multi->warned = true;\n  }\n}\n#else\n#define multi_warn_debug(x,y) Curl_nop_stmt\n#endif\n\n/* returns TRUE if the easy handle is supposed to be present in the main link\n   list */\nstatic bool in_main_list(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  return ((data->mstate != MSTATE_PENDING) &&\n          (data->mstate != MSTATE_MSGSENT));\n}\n\nstatic void link_easy(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n                      struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  /* We add the new easy entry last in the list. */\n  data->next = NULL; /* end of the line */\n  if(multi->easyp) {\n    struct Curl_easy *last = multi->easylp;\n    last->next = data;\n    data->prev = last;\n    multi->easylp = data; /* the new last node */\n  }\n  else {\n    /* first node, make prev NULL! */\n    data->prev = NULL;\n    multi->easylp = multi->easyp = data; /* both first and last */\n  }\n}\n\n/* unlink the given easy handle from the linked list of easy handles */\nstatic void unlink_easy(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n                        struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  /* make the previous node point to our next */\n  if(data->prev)\n    data->prev->next = data->next;\n  else\n    multi->easyp = data->next; /* point to first node */\n\n  /* make our next point to our previous node */\n  if(data->next)\n    data->next->prev = data->prev;\n  else\n    multi->easylp = data->prev; /* point to last node */\n\n  data->prev = data->next = NULL;\n}\n\n\nCURLMcode curl_multi_add_handle(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n                                struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  CURLMcode rc;\n  /* First, make some basic checks that the CURLM handle is a good handle */\n  if(!GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE(multi))\n    return CURLM_BAD_HANDLE;\n\n  /* Verify that we got a somewhat good easy handle too */\n  if(!GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(data))\n    return CURLM_BAD_EASY_HANDLE;\n\n  /* Prevent users from adding same easy handle more than once and prevent\n     adding to more than one multi stack */\n  if(data->multi)\n    return CURLM_ADDED_ALREADY;\n\n  if(multi->in_callback)\n    return CURLM_RECURSIVE_API_CALL;\n\n  if(multi->dead) {\n    /* a \"dead\" handle cannot get added transfers while any existing easy\n       handles are still alive - but if there are none alive anymore, it is\n       fine to start over and unmark the \"deadness\" of this handle */\n    if(multi->num_alive)\n      return CURLM_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK;\n    multi->dead = FALSE;\n  }\n\n  /* Initialize timeout list for this handle */\n  Curl_llist_init(&data->state.timeoutlist, NULL);\n\n  /*\n   * No failure allowed in this function beyond this point. And no\n   * modification of easy nor multi handle allowed before this except for\n   * potential multi's connection cache growing which won't be undone in this\n   * function no matter what.\n   */\n  if(data->set.errorbuffer)\n    data->set.errorbuffer[0] = 0;\n\n  /* make the Curl_easy refer back to this multi handle - before Curl_expire()\n     is called. */\n  data->multi = multi;\n\n  /* Set the timeout for this handle to expire really soon so that it will\n     be taken care of even when this handle is added in the midst of operation\n     when only the curl_multi_socket() API is used. During that flow, only\n     sockets that time-out or have actions will be dealt with. Since this\n     handle has no action yet, we make sure it times out to get things to\n     happen. */\n  Curl_expire(data, 0, EXPIRE_RUN_NOW);\n\n  /* A somewhat crude work-around for a little glitch in Curl_update_timer()\n     that happens if the lastcall time is set to the same time when the handle\n     is removed as when the next handle is added, as then the check in\n     Curl_update_timer() that prevents calling the application multiple times\n     with the same timer info will not trigger and then the new handle's\n     timeout will not be notified to the app.\n\n     The work-around is thus simply to clear the 'lastcall' variable to force\n     Curl_update_timer() to always trigger a callback to the app when a new\n     easy handle is added */\n  memset(&multi->timer_lastcall, 0, sizeof(multi->timer_lastcall));\n\n  rc = Curl_update_timer(multi);\n  if(rc)\n    return rc;\n\n  /* set the easy handle */\n  multistate(data, MSTATE_INIT);\n\n  /* for multi interface connections, we share DNS cache automatically if the\n     easy handle's one is currently not set. */\n  if(!data->dns.hostcache ||\n     (data->dns.hostcachetype == HCACHE_NONE)) {\n    data->dns.hostcache = &multi->hostcache;\n    data->dns.hostcachetype = HCACHE_MULTI;\n  }\n\n  /* Point to the shared or multi handle connection cache */\n  if(data->share && (data->share->specifier & (1<< CURL_LOCK_DATA_CONNECT)))\n    data->state.conn_cache = &data->share->conn_cache;\n  else\n    data->state.conn_cache = &multi->conn_cache;\n  data->state.lastconnect_id = -1;\n\n#ifdef USE_LIBPSL\n  /* Do the same for PSL. */\n  if(data->share && (data->share->specifier & (1 << CURL_LOCK_DATA_PSL)))\n    data->psl = &data->share->psl;\n  else\n    data->psl = &multi->psl;\n#endif\n\n  link_easy(multi, data);\n\n  /* increase the node-counter */\n  multi->num_easy++;\n\n  /* increase the alive-counter */\n  multi->num_alive++;\n\n  CONNCACHE_LOCK(data);\n  /* The closure handle only ever has default timeouts set. To improve the\n     state somewhat we clone the timeouts from each added handle so that the\n     closure handle always has the same timeouts as the most recently added\n     easy handle. */\n  data->state.conn_cache->closure_handle->set.timeout = data->set.timeout;\n  data->state.conn_cache->closure_handle->set.server_response_timeout =\n    data->set.server_response_timeout;\n  data->state.conn_cache->closure_handle->set.no_signal =\n    data->set.no_signal;\n  data->id = data->state.conn_cache->next_easy_id++;\n  if(data->state.conn_cache->next_easy_id <= 0)\n    data->state.conn_cache->next_easy_id = 0;\n  CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\n\n  multi_warn_debug(multi, data);\n\n  return CURLM_OK;\n}\n\n#if 0\n/* Debug-function, used like this:\n *\n * Curl_hash_print(&multi->sockhash, debug_print_sock_hash);\n *\n * Enable the hash print function first by editing hash.c\n */\nstatic void debug_print_sock_hash(void *p)\n{\n  struct Curl_sh_entry *sh = (struct Curl_sh_entry *)p;\n\n  fprintf(stderr, \" [readers %u][writers %u]\",\n          sh->readers, sh->writers);\n}\n#endif\n\nstatic CURLcode multi_done(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                           CURLcode status,  /* an error if this is called\n                                                after an error was detected */\n                           bool premature)\n{\n  CURLcode result;\n  struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n\n#if defined(DEBUGBUILD) && !defined(CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS)\n  DEBUGF(infof(data, \"multi_done[%s]: status: %d prem: %d done: %d\",\n               multi_statename[data->mstate],\n               (int)status, (int)premature, data->state.done));\n#else\n  DEBUGF(infof(data, \"multi_done: status: %d prem: %d done: %d\",\n               (int)status, (int)premature, data->state.done));\n#endif\n\n  if(data->state.done)\n    /* Stop if multi_done() has already been called */\n    return CURLE_OK;\n\n  /* Stop the resolver and free its own resources (but not dns_entry yet). */\n  Curl_resolver_kill(data);\n\n  /* Cleanup possible redirect junk */\n  Curl_safefree(data->req.newurl);\n  Curl_safefree(data->req.location);\n\n  switch(status) {\n  case CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK:\n  case CURLE_READ_ERROR:\n  case CURLE_WRITE_ERROR:\n    /* When we're aborted due to a callback return code it basically have to\n       be counted as premature as there is trouble ahead if we don't. We have\n       many callbacks and protocols work differently, we could potentially do\n       this more fine-grained in the future. */\n    premature = TRUE;\n  default:\n    break;\n  }\n\n  /* this calls the protocol-specific function pointer previously set */\n  if(conn->handler->done)\n    result = conn->handler->done(data, status, premature);\n  else\n    result = status;\n\n  if(CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK != result) {\n    /* avoid this if we already aborted by callback to avoid this calling\n       another callback */\n    int rc = Curl_pgrsDone(data);\n    if(!result && rc)\n      result = CURLE_ABORTED_BY_CALLBACK;\n  }\n\n  /* Inform connection filters that this transfer is done */\n  Curl_conn_ev_data_done(data, premature);\n\n  multi_activate_pending(data->multi); /* connection / multiplex */\n\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.ulbuf);\n\n  Curl_client_cleanup(data);\n\n  CONNCACHE_LOCK(data);\n  Curl_detach_connection(data);\n  if(CONN_INUSE(conn)) {\n    /* Stop if still used. */\n    CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\n    DEBUGF(infof(data, \"Connection still in use %zu, \"\n                 \"no more multi_done now!\",\n                 conn->easyq.size));\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n\n  data->state.done = TRUE; /* called just now! */\n\n  if(conn->dns_entry) {\n    Curl_resolv_unlock(data, conn->dns_entry); /* done with this */\n    conn->dns_entry = NULL;\n  }\n  Curl_hostcache_prune(data);\n\n  /* if data->set.reuse_forbid is TRUE, it means the libcurl client has\n     forced us to close this connection. This is ignored for requests taking\n     place in a NTLM/NEGOTIATE authentication handshake\n\n     if conn->bits.close is TRUE, it means that the connection should be\n     closed in spite of all our efforts to be nice, due to protocol\n     restrictions in our or the server's end\n\n     if premature is TRUE, it means this connection was said to be DONE before\n     the entire request operation is complete and thus we can't know in what\n     state it is for reusing, so we're forced to close it. In a perfect world\n     we can add code that keep track of if we really must close it here or not,\n     but currently we have no such detail knowledge.\n  */\n\n  data->state.recent_conn_id = conn->connection_id;\n  if((data->set.reuse_forbid\n#if defined(USE_NTLM)\n      && !(conn->http_ntlm_state == NTLMSTATE_TYPE2 ||\n           conn->proxy_ntlm_state == NTLMSTATE_TYPE2)\n#endif\n#if defined(USE_SPNEGO)\n      && !(conn->http_negotiate_state == GSS_AUTHRECV ||\n           conn->proxy_negotiate_state == GSS_AUTHRECV)\n#endif\n     ) || conn->bits.close\n       || (premature && !Curl_conn_is_multiplex(conn, FIRSTSOCKET))) {\n    DEBUGF(infof(data, \"multi_done, not reusing connection=%\"\n                       CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T \", forbid=%d\"\n                       \", close=%d, premature=%d, conn_multiplex=%d\",\n                 conn->connection_id,\n                 data->set.reuse_forbid, conn->bits.close, premature,\n                 Curl_conn_is_multiplex(conn, FIRSTSOCKET)));\n    connclose(conn, \"disconnecting\");\n    Curl_conncache_remove_conn(data, conn, FALSE);\n    CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\n    Curl_disconnect(data, conn, premature);\n  }\n  else {\n    char buffer[256];\n    const char *host =\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n      conn->bits.socksproxy ?\n      conn->socks_proxy.host.dispname :\n      conn->bits.httpproxy ? conn->http_proxy.host.dispname :\n#endif\n      conn->bits.conn_to_host ? conn->conn_to_host.dispname :\n      conn->host.dispname;\n    /* create string before returning the connection */\n    curl_off_t connection_id = conn->connection_id;\n    msnprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer),\n              \"Connection #%\" CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T \" to host %s left intact\",\n              connection_id, host);\n    /* the connection is no longer in use by this transfer */\n    CONNCACHE_UNLOCK(data);\n    if(Curl_conncache_return_conn(data, conn)) {\n      /* remember the most recently used connection */\n      data->state.lastconnect_id = connection_id;\n      data->state.recent_conn_id = connection_id;\n      infof(data, \"%s\", buffer);\n    }\n    else\n      data->state.lastconnect_id = -1;\n  }\n\n  Curl_safefree(data->state.buffer);\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic int close_connect_only(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                              struct connectdata *conn, void *param)\n{\n  (void)param;\n  if(data->state.lastconnect_id != conn->connection_id)\n    return 0;\n\n  if(!conn->connect_only)\n    return 1;\n\n  connclose(conn, \"Removing connect-only easy handle\");\n\n  return 1;\n}\n\nCURLMcode curl_multi_remove_handle(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n                                   struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct Curl_easy *easy = data;\n  bool premature;\n  struct Curl_llist_element *e;\n  CURLMcode rc;\n\n  /* First, make some basic checks that the CURLM handle is a good handle */\n  if(!GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE(multi))\n    return CURLM_BAD_HANDLE;\n\n  /* Verify that we got a somewhat good easy handle too */\n  if(!GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(data))\n    return CURLM_BAD_EASY_HANDLE;\n\n  /* Prevent users from trying to remove same easy handle more than once */\n  if(!data->multi)\n    return CURLM_OK; /* it is already removed so let's say it is fine! */\n\n  /* Prevent users from trying to remove an easy handle from the wrong multi */\n  if(data->multi != multi)\n    return CURLM_BAD_EASY_HANDLE;\n\n  if(multi->in_callback)\n    return CURLM_RECURSIVE_API_CALL;\n\n  premature = (data->mstate < MSTATE_COMPLETED) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n\n  /* If the 'state' is not INIT or COMPLETED, we might need to do something\n     nice to put the easy_handle in a good known state when this returns. */\n  if(premature) {\n    /* this handle is \"alive\" so we need to count down the total number of\n       alive connections when this is removed */\n    multi->num_alive--;\n  }\n\n  if(data->conn &&\n     data->mstate > MSTATE_DO &&\n     data->mstate < MSTATE_COMPLETED) {\n    /* Set connection owner so that the DONE function closes it.  We can\n       safely do this here since connection is killed. */\n    streamclose(data->conn, \"Removed with partial response\");\n  }\n\n  if(data->conn) {\n    /* multi_done() clears the association between the easy handle and the\n       connection.\n\n       Note that this ignores the return code simply because there's\n       nothing really useful to do with it anyway! */\n    (void)multi_done(data, data->result, premature);\n  }\n\n  /* The timer must be shut down before data->multi is set to NULL, else the\n     timenode will remain in the splay tree after curl_easy_cleanup is\n     called. Do it after multi_done() in case that sets another time! */\n  Curl_expire_clear(data);\n\n  if(data->connect_queue.ptr) {\n    /* the handle is in the pending or msgsent lists, so go ahead and remove\n       it */\n    if(data->mstate == MSTATE_PENDING)\n      Curl_llist_remove(&multi->pending, &data->connect_queue, NULL);\n    else\n      Curl_llist_remove(&multi->msgsent, &data->connect_queue, NULL);\n  }\n  if(in_main_list(data))\n    unlink_easy(multi, data);\n\n  if(data->dns.hostcachetype == HCACHE_MULTI) {\n    /* stop using the multi handle's DNS cache, *after* the possible\n       multi_done() call above */\n    data->dns.hostcache = NULL;\n    data->dns.hostcachetype = HCACHE_NONE;\n  }\n\n  Curl_wildcard_dtor(&data->wildcard);\n\n  /* change state without using multistate(), only to make singlesocket() do\n     what we want */\n  data->mstate = MSTATE_COMPLETED;\n\n  /* This ignores the return code even in case of problems because there's\n     nothing more to do about that, here */\n  (void)singlesocket(multi, easy); /* to let the application know what sockets\n                                      that vanish with this handle */\n\n  /* Remove the association between the connection and the handle */\n  Curl_detach_connection(data);\n\n  if(data->set.connect_only && !data->multi_easy) {\n    /* This removes a handle that was part the multi interface that used\n       CONNECT_ONLY, that connection is now left alive but since this handle\n       has bits.close set nothing can use that transfer anymore and it is\n       forbidden from reuse. And this easy handle cannot find the connection\n       anymore once removed from the multi handle\n\n       Better close the connection here, at once.\n    */\n    struct connectdata *c;\n    curl_socket_t s;\n    s = Curl_getconnectinfo(data, &c);\n    if((s != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) && c) {\n      Curl_conncache_remove_conn(data, c, TRUE);\n      Curl_disconnect(data, c, TRUE);\n    }\n  }\n\n  if(data->state.lastconnect_id != -1) {\n    /* Mark any connect-only connection for closure */\n    Curl_conncache_foreach(data, data->state.conn_cache,\n                           NULL, close_connect_only);\n  }\n\n#ifdef USE_LIBPSL\n  /* Remove the PSL association. */\n  if(data->psl == &multi->psl)\n    data->psl = NULL;\n#endif\n\n  /* as this was using a shared connection cache we clear the pointer to that\n     since we're not part of that multi handle anymore */\n  data->state.conn_cache = NULL;\n\n  data->multi = NULL; /* clear the association to this multi handle */\n\n  /* make sure there's no pending message in the queue sent from this easy\n     handle */\n  for(e = multi->msglist.head; e; e = e->next) {\n    struct Curl_message *msg = e->ptr;\n\n    if(msg->extmsg.easy_handle == easy) {\n      Curl_llist_remove(&multi->msglist, e, NULL);\n      /* there can only be one from this specific handle */\n      break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  /* NOTE NOTE NOTE\n     We do not touch the easy handle here! */\n  multi->num_easy--; /* one less to care about now */\n\n  multi_activate_pending(multi);\n\n  rc = Curl_update_timer(multi);\n  if(rc)\n    return rc;\n  return CURLM_OK;\n}\n\n/* Return TRUE if the application asked for multiplexing */\nbool Curl_multiplex_wanted(const struct Curl_multi *multi)\n{\n  return (multi && (multi->multiplexing));\n}\n\n/*\n * Curl_detach_connection() removes the given transfer from the connection.\n *\n * This is the only function that should clear data->conn. This will\n * occasionally be called with the data->conn pointer already cleared.\n */\nvoid Curl_detach_connection(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n  if(conn) {\n    Curl_conn_ev_data_detach(conn, data);\n    Curl_llist_remove(&conn->easyq, &data->conn_queue, NULL);\n  }\n  data->conn = NULL;\n}\n\n/*\n * Curl_attach_connection() attaches this transfer to this connection.\n *\n * This is the only function that should assign data->conn\n */\nvoid Curl_attach_connection(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                             struct connectdata *conn)\n{\n  DEBUGASSERT(!data->conn);\n  DEBUGASSERT(conn);\n  data->conn = conn;\n  Curl_llist_insert_next(&conn->easyq, conn->easyq.tail, data,\n                         &data->conn_queue);\n  if(conn->handler && conn->handler->attach)\n    conn->handler->attach(data, conn);\n  Curl_conn_ev_data_attach(conn, data);\n}\n\nstatic int domore_getsock(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                          struct connectdata *conn,\n                          curl_socket_t *socks)\n{\n  if(conn && conn->handler->domore_getsock)\n    return conn->handler->domore_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n  return GETSOCK_BLANK;\n}\n\nstatic int doing_getsock(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                         struct connectdata *conn,\n                         curl_socket_t *socks)\n{\n  if(conn && conn->handler->doing_getsock)\n    return conn->handler->doing_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n  return GETSOCK_BLANK;\n}\n\nstatic int protocol_getsock(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                            struct connectdata *conn,\n                            curl_socket_t *socks)\n{\n  if(conn->handler->proto_getsock)\n    return conn->handler->proto_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n  return Curl_conn_get_select_socks(data, FIRSTSOCKET, socks);\n}\n\n/* returns bitmapped flags for this handle and its sockets. The 'socks[]'\n   array contains MAX_SOCKSPEREASYHANDLE entries. */\nstatic int multi_getsock(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                         curl_socket_t *socks)\n{\n  struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n  /* The no connection case can happen when this is called from\n     curl_multi_remove_handle() => singlesocket() => multi_getsock().\n  */\n  if(!conn)\n    return 0;\n\n  switch(data->mstate) {\n  default:\n    return 0;\n\n  case MSTATE_RESOLVING:\n    return Curl_resolv_getsock(data, socks);\n\n  case MSTATE_PROTOCONNECTING:\n  case MSTATE_PROTOCONNECT:\n    return protocol_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n\n  case MSTATE_DO:\n  case MSTATE_DOING:\n    return doing_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n\n  case MSTATE_TUNNELING:\n  case MSTATE_CONNECTING:\n    return Curl_conn_get_select_socks(data, FIRSTSOCKET, socks);\n\n  case MSTATE_DOING_MORE:\n    return domore_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n\n  case MSTATE_DID: /* since is set after DO is completed, we switch to\n                        waiting for the same as the PERFORMING state */\n  case MSTATE_PERFORMING:\n    return Curl_single_getsock(data, conn, socks);\n  }\n\n}\n\nCURLMcode curl_multi_fdset(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n                           fd_set *read_fd_set, fd_set *write_fd_set,\n                           fd_set *exc_fd_set, int *max_fd)\n{\n  /* Scan through all the easy handles to get the file descriptors set.\n     Some easy handles may not have connected to the remote host yet,\n     and then we must make sure that is done. */\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n  int this_max_fd = -1;\n  curl_socket_t sockbunch[MAX_SOCKSPEREASYHANDLE];\n  int i;\n  (void)exc_fd_set; /* not used */\n\n  if(!GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE(multi))\n    return CURLM_BAD_HANDLE;\n\n  if(multi->in_callback)\n    return CURLM_RECURSIVE_API_CALL;\n\n  for(data = multi->easyp; data; data = data->next) {\n    int bitmap;\n#ifdef __clang_analyzer_\n    /* to prevent \"The left operand of '>=' is a garbage value\" warnings */\n    memset(sockbunch, 0, sizeof(sockbunch));\n#endif\n    bitmap = multi_getsock(data, sockbunch);\n\n    for(i = 0; i< MAX_SOCKSPEREASYHANDLE; i++) {\n      if((bitmap & GETSOCK_MASK_RW(i)) && VALID_SOCK((sockbunch[i]))) {\n        if(!FDSET_SOCK(sockbunch[i]))\n          /* pretend it doesn't exist */\n          continue;\n        if(bitmap & GETSOCK_READSOCK(i))\n          FD_SET(sockbunch[i], read_fd_set);\n        if(bitmap & GETSOCK_WRITESOCK(i))\n          FD_SET(sockbunch[i], write_fd_set);\n        if((int)sockbunch[i] > this_max_fd)\n          this_max_fd = (int)sockbunch[i];\n      }\n      else {\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  *max_fd = this_max_fd;\n\n  return CURLM_OK;\n}\n\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n/* Reset FD_WRITE for TCP sockets. Nothing is actually sent. UDP sockets can't\n * be reset this way because an empty datagram would be sent. #9203\n *\n * \"On Windows the internal state of FD_WRITE as returned from\n * WSAEnumNetworkEvents is only reset after successful send().\"\n */\nstatic void reset_socket_fdwrite(curl_socket_t s)\n{\n  int t;\n  int l = (int)sizeof(t);\n  if(!getsockopt(s, SOL_SOCKET, SO_TYPE, (char *)&t, &l) && t == SOCK_STREAM)\n    send(s, NULL, 0, 0);\n}\n#endif\n\n#define NUM_POLLS_ON_STACK 10\n\nstatic CURLMcode multi_wait(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n                            struct curl_waitfd extra_fds[],\n                            unsigned int extra_nfds,\n                            int timeout_ms,\n                            int *ret,\n                            bool extrawait, /* when no socket, wait */\n                            bool use_wakeup)\n{\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n  curl_socket_t sockbunch[MAX_SOCKSPEREASYHANDLE];\n  int bitmap;\n  unsigned int i;\n  unsigned int nfds = 0;\n  unsigned int curlfds;\n  long timeout_internal;\n  int retcode = 0;\n  struct pollfd a_few_on_stack[NUM_POLLS_ON_STACK];\n  struct pollfd *ufds = &a_few_on_stack[0];\n  bool ufds_malloc = FALSE;\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n  WSANETWORKEVENTS wsa_events;\n  DEBUGASSERT(multi->wsa_event != WSA_INVALID_EVENT);\n#endif\n#ifndef ENABLE_WAKEUP\n  (void)use_wakeup;\n#endif\n\n  if(!GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE(multi))\n    return CURLM_BAD_HANDLE;\n\n  if(multi->in_callback)\n    return CURLM_RECURSIVE_API_CALL;\n\n  if(timeout_ms < 0)\n    return CURLM_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n  /* Count up how many fds we have from the multi handle */\n  for(data = multi->easyp; data; data = data->next) {\n    bitmap = multi_getsock(data, sockbunch);\n\n    for(i = 0; i < MAX_SOCKSPEREASYHANDLE; i++) {\n      if((bitmap & GETSOCK_MASK_RW(i)) && VALID_SOCK((sockbunch[i]))) {\n        ++nfds;\n      }\n      else {\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  /* If the internally desired timeout is actually shorter than requested from\n     the outside, then use the shorter time! But only if the internal timer\n     is actually larger than -1! */\n  (void)multi_timeout(multi, &timeout_internal);\n  if((timeout_internal >= 0) && (timeout_internal < (long)timeout_ms))\n    timeout_ms = (int)timeout_internal;\n\n  curlfds = nfds; /* number of internal file descriptors */\n  nfds += extra_nfds; /* add the externally provided ones */\n\n#ifdef ENABLE_WAKEUP\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n  if(use_wakeup) {\n#else\n  if(use_wakeup && multi->wakeup_pair[0] != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) {\n#endif\n    ++nfds;\n  }\n#endif\n\n  if(nfds > NUM_POLLS_ON_STACK) {\n    /* 'nfds' is a 32 bit value and 'struct pollfd' is typically 8 bytes\n       big, so at 2^29 sockets this value might wrap. When a process gets\n       the capability to actually handle over 500 million sockets this\n       calculation needs a integer overflow check. */\n    ufds = malloc(nfds * sizeof(struct pollfd));\n    if(!ufds)\n      return CURLM_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    ufds_malloc = TRUE;\n  }\n  nfds = 0;\n\n  /* only do the second loop if we found descriptors in the first stage run\n     above */\n\n  if(curlfds) {\n    /* Add the curl handles to our pollfds first */\n    for(data = multi->easyp; data; data = data->next) {\n      bitmap = multi_getsock(data, sockbunch);\n\n      for(i = 0; i < MAX_SOCKSPEREASYHANDLE; i++) {\n        if((bitmap & GETSOCK_MASK_RW(i)) && VALID_SOCK((sockbunch[i]))) {\n          struct pollfd *ufd = &ufds[nfds++];\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n          long mask = 0;\n#endif\n          ufd->fd = sockbunch[i];\n          ufd->events = 0;\n          if(bitmap & GETSOCK_READSOCK(i)) {\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n            mask |= FD_READ|FD_ACCEPT|FD_CLOSE;\n#endif\n            ufd->events |= POLLIN;\n          }\n          if(bitmap & GETSOCK_WRITESOCK(i)) {\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n            mask |= FD_WRITE|FD_CONNECT|FD_CLOSE;\n            reset_socket_fdwrite(sockbunch[i]);\n#endif\n            ufd->events |= POLLOUT;\n          }\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n          if(WSAEventSelect(sockbunch[i], multi->wsa_event, mask) != 0) {\n            if(ufds_malloc)\n              free(ufds);\n            return CURLM_INTERNAL_ERROR;\n          }\n#endif\n        }\n        else {\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  /* Add external file descriptions from poll-like struct curl_waitfd */\n  for(i = 0; i < extra_nfds; i++) {\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n    long mask = 0;\n    if(extra_fds[i].events & CURL_WAIT_POLLIN)\n      mask |= FD_READ|FD_ACCEPT|FD_CLOSE;\n    if(extra_fds[i].events & CURL_WAIT_POLLPRI)\n      mask |= FD_OOB;\n    if(extra_fds[i].events & CURL_WAIT_POLLOUT) {\n      mask |= FD_WRITE|FD_CONNECT|FD_CLOSE;\n      reset_socket_fdwrite(extra_fds[i].fd);\n    }\n    if(WSAEventSelect(extra_fds[i].fd, multi->wsa_event, mask) != 0) {\n      if(ufds_malloc)\n        free(ufds);\n      return CURLM_INTERNAL_ERROR;\n    }\n#endif\n    ufds[nfds].fd = extra_fds[i].fd;\n    ufds[nfds].events = 0;\n    if(extra_fds[i].events & CURL_WAIT_POLLIN)\n      ufds[nfds].events |= POLLIN;\n    if(extra_fds[i].events & CURL_WAIT_POLLPRI)\n      ufds[nfds].events |= POLLPRI;\n    if(extra_fds[i].events & CURL_WAIT_POLLOUT)\n      ufds[nfds].events |= POLLOUT;\n    ++nfds;\n  }\n\n#ifdef ENABLE_WAKEUP\n#ifndef USE_WINSOCK\n  if(use_wakeup && multi->wakeup_pair[0] != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) {\n    ufds[nfds].fd = multi->wakeup_pair[0];\n    ufds[nfds].events = POLLIN;\n    ++nfds;\n  }\n#endif\n#endif\n\n#if defined(ENABLE_WAKEUP) && defined(USE_WINSOCK)\n  if(nfds || use_wakeup) {\n#else\n  if(nfds) {\n#endif\n    int pollrc;\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n    if(nfds)\n      pollrc = Curl_poll(ufds, nfds, 0); /* just pre-check with WinSock */\n    else\n      pollrc = 0;\n#else\n    pollrc = Curl_poll(ufds, nfds, timeout_ms); /* wait... */\n#endif\n    if(pollrc < 0)\n      return CURLM_UNRECOVERABLE_POLL;\n\n    if(pollrc > 0) {\n      retcode = pollrc;\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n    }\n    else { /* now wait... if not ready during the pre-check (pollrc == 0) */\n      WSAWaitForMultipleEvents(1, &multi->wsa_event, FALSE, timeout_ms, FALSE);\n    }\n    /* With WinSock, we have to run the following section unconditionally\n       to call WSAEventSelect(fd, event, 0) on all the sockets */\n    {\n#endif\n      /* copy revents results from the poll to the curl_multi_wait poll\n         struct, the bit values of the actual underlying poll() implementation\n         may not be the same as the ones in the public libcurl API! */\n      for(i = 0; i < extra_nfds; i++) {\n        unsigned r = ufds[curlfds + i].revents;\n        unsigned short mask = 0;\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n        curl_socket_t s = extra_fds[i].fd;\n        wsa_events.lNetworkEvents = 0;\n        if(WSAEnumNetworkEvents(s, NULL, &wsa_events) == 0) {\n          if(wsa_events.lNetworkEvents & (FD_READ|FD_ACCEPT|FD_CLOSE))\n            mask |= CURL_WAIT_POLLIN;\n          if(wsa_events.lNetworkEvents & (FD_WRITE|FD_CONNECT|FD_CLOSE))\n            mask |= CURL_WAIT_POLLOUT;\n          if(wsa_events.lNetworkEvents & FD_OOB)\n            mask |= CURL_WAIT_POLLPRI;\n          if(ret && !pollrc && wsa_events.lNetworkEvents)\n            retcode++;\n        }\n        WSAEventSelect(s, multi->wsa_event, 0);\n        if(!pollrc) {\n          extra_fds[i].revents = mask;\n          continue;\n        }\n#endif\n        if(r & POLLIN)\n          mask |= CURL_WAIT_POLLIN;\n        if(r & POLLOUT)\n          mask |= CURL_WAIT_POLLOUT;\n        if(r & POLLPRI)\n          mask |= CURL_WAIT_POLLPRI;\n        extra_fds[i].revents = mask;\n      }\n\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n      /* Count up all our own sockets that had activity,\n         and remove them from the event. */\n      if(curlfds) {\n\n        for(data = multi->easyp; data; data = data->next) {\n          bitmap = multi_getsock(data, sockbunch);\n\n          for(i = 0; i < MAX_SOCKSPEREASYHANDLE; i++) {\n            if(bitmap & (GETSOCK_READSOCK(i) | GETSOCK_WRITESOCK(i))) {\n              wsa_events.lNetworkEvents = 0;\n              if(WSAEnumNetworkEvents(sockbunch[i], NULL, &wsa_events) == 0) {\n                if(ret && !pollrc && wsa_events.lNetworkEvents)\n                  retcode++;\n              }\n              WSAEventSelect(sockbunch[i], multi->wsa_event, 0);\n            }\n            else {\n              /* break on entry not checked for being readable or writable */\n              break;\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      WSAResetEvent(multi->wsa_event);\n#else\n#ifdef ENABLE_WAKEUP\n      if(use_wakeup && multi->wakeup_pair[0] != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) {\n        if(ufds[curlfds + extra_nfds].revents & POLLIN) {\n          char buf[64];\n          ssize_t nread;\n          while(1) {\n            /* the reading socket is non-blocking, try to read\n               data from it until it receives an error (except EINTR).\n               In normal cases it will get EAGAIN or EWOULDBLOCK\n               when there is no more data, breaking the loop. */\n            nread = wakeup_read(multi->wakeup_pair[0], buf, sizeof(buf));\n            if(nread <= 0) {\n              if(nread < 0 && EINTR == SOCKERRNO)\n                continue;\n              break;\n            }\n          }\n          /* do not count the wakeup socket into the returned value */\n          retcode--;\n        }\n      }\n#endif\n#endif\n    }\n  }\n\n  if(ufds_malloc)\n    free(ufds);\n  if(ret)\n    *ret = retcode;\n#if defined(ENABLE_WAKEUP) && defined(USE_WINSOCK)\n  if(extrawait && !nfds && !use_wakeup) {\n#else\n  if(extrawait && !nfds) {\n#endif\n    long sleep_ms = 0;\n\n    /* Avoid busy-looping when there's nothing particular to wait for */\n    if(!curl_multi_timeout(multi, &sleep_ms) && sleep_ms) {\n      if(sleep_ms > timeout_ms)\n        sleep_ms = timeout_ms;\n      /* when there are no easy handles in the multi, this holds a -1\n         timeout */\n      else if(sleep_ms < 0)\n        sleep_ms = timeout_ms;\n      Curl_wait_ms(sleep_ms);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return CURLM_OK;\n}\n\nCURLMcode curl_multi_wait(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n                          struct curl_waitfd extra_fds[],\n                          unsigned int extra_nfds,\n                          int timeout_ms,\n                          int *ret)\n{\n  return multi_wait(multi, extra_fds, extra_nfds, timeout_ms, ret, FALSE,\n                    FALSE);\n}\n\nCURLMcode curl_multi_poll(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n                          struct curl_waitfd extra_fds[],\n                          unsigned int extra_nfds,\n                          int timeout_ms,\n                          int *ret)\n{\n  return multi_wait(multi, extra_fds, extra_nfds, timeout_ms, ret, TRUE,\n                    TRUE);\n}\n\nCURLMcode curl_multi_wakeup(struct Curl_multi *multi)\n{\n  /* this function is usually called from another thread,\n     it has to be careful only to access parts of the\n     Curl_multi struct that are constant */\n\n  /* GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE can be safely called */\n  if(!GOOD_MULTI_HANDLE(multi))\n    return CURLM_BAD_HANDLE;\n\n#ifdef ENABLE_WAKEUP\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n  if(WSASetEvent(multi->wsa_event))\n    return CURLM_OK;\n#else\n  /* the wakeup_pair variable is only written during init and cleanup,\n     making it safe to access from another thread after the init part\n     and before cleanup */\n  if(multi->wakeup_pair[1] != CURL_SOCKET_BAD) {\n    char buf[1];\n    buf[0] = 1;\n    while(1) {\n      /* swrite() is not thread-safe in general, because concurrent calls\n         can have their messages interleaved, but in this case the content\n         of the messages does not matter, which makes it ok to call.\n\n         The write socket is set to non-blocking, this way this function\n         cannot block, making it safe to call even from the same thread\n         that will call curl_multi_wait(). If swrite() returns that it\n         would block, it's considered successful because it means that\n         previous calls to this function will wake up the poll(). */\n      if(wakeup_write(multi->wakeup_pair[1], buf, sizeof(buf)) < 0) {\n        int err = SOCKERRNO;\n        int return_success;\n#ifdef USE_WINSOCK\n        return_success = WSAEWOULDBLOCK == err;\n#else\n        if(EINTR == err)\n          continue;\n        return_success = EWOULDBLOCK == err || EAGAIN == err;\n#endif\n        if(!return_success)\n          return CURLM_WAKEUP_FAILURE;\n      }\n      return CURLM_OK;\n    }\n  }\n#endif\n#endif\n  return CURLM_WAKEUP_FAILURE;\n}\n\n/*\n * multi_ischanged() is called\n *\n * Returns TRUE/FALSE whether the state is changed to trigger a CONNECT_PEND\n * => CONNECT action.\n *\n * Set 'clear' to TRUE to have it also clear the state variable.\n */\nstatic bool multi_ischanged(struct Curl_multi *multi, bool clear)\n{\n  bool retval = multi->recheckstate;\n  if(clear)\n    multi->recheckstate = FALSE;\n  return retval;\n}\n\n/*\n * Curl_multi_connchanged() is called to tell that there is a connection in\n * this multi handle that has changed state (multiplexing become possible, the\n * number of allowed streams changed or similar), and a subsequent use of this\n * multi handle should move CONNECT_PEND handles back to CONNECT to have them\n * retry.\n */\nvoid Curl_multi_connchanged(struct Curl_multi *multi)\n{\n  multi->recheckstate = TRUE;\n}\n\nCURLMcode Curl_multi_add_perform(struct Curl_multi *multi,\n                                 struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                 struct connectdata *conn)\n{\n  CURLMcode rc;\n\n  if(multi->in_callback)\n    return CURLM_RECURSIVE_API_CALL;\n\n  rc = curl_multi_add_handle(multi, data);\n  if(!rc) {\n    struct SingleRequest *k = &data->req;\n\n    /* pass in NULL for 'conn' here since we don't want to init the\n       connection, only this transfer */\n    Curl_init_do(data, NULL);\n\n    /* take this handle to the perform state right away */\n    multistate(data, MSTATE_PERFORMING);\n    Curl_attach_connection(data, conn);\n    k->keepon |= KEEP_RECV; /* setup to receive! */\n  }\n  return rc;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode multi_do(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(conn);\n  DEBUGASSERT(conn->handler);\n\n  if(conn->handler->do_it)\n    result = conn->handler->do_it(data, done);\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n/*\n * multi_do_more() is called during the DO_MORE multi state. It is basically a\n * second stage DO state which (wrongly) was introduced to support FTP's\n * second connection.\n *\n * 'complete' can return 0 for incomplete, 1 for done and -1 for go back to\n * DOING state there's more work to do!\n */\n\nstatic CURLcode multi_do_more(struct Curl_easy *data, int *complete)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n\n  *complete = 0;\n\n  if(conn->handler->do_more)\n    result = conn->handler->do_more(data, complete);\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n/*\n * Check whether a timeout occurred, and handle it if it did\n */\nstatic bool multi_handle_timeout(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                 struct curltime *now,\n                                 bool *stream_error,\n                                 CURLcode *result,\n                                 bool connect_timeout)\n{\n  timediff_t timeout_ms;\n  timeout_ms = Curl_timeleft(data, now, connect_timeout);\n\n  if(timeout_ms < 0) {\n    /* Handle timed out */\n    if(data->mstate == MSTATE_RESOLVING)\n      failf(data, \"Resolving timed out after %\" CURL_FORMAT_TIMEDIFF_T\n            \" milliseconds\",\n            Curl_timediff(*now, data->progress.t_startsingle));\n    else if(data->mstate == MSTATE_CONNECTING)\n      failf(data, \"Connection timed out after %\" CURL_FORMAT_TIMEDIFF_T\n            \" milliseconds\",\n            Curl_timediff(*now, data->progress.t_startsingle));\n    else {\n      struct SingleRequest *k = &data->req;\n      if(k->size != -1) {\n        failf(data, \"Operation timed out after %\" CURL_FORMAT_TIMEDIFF_T\n              \" milliseconds with %\" CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T \" out of %\"\n              CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T \" bytes received\",\n              Curl_timediff(*now, data->progress.t_startsingle),\n              k->bytecount, k->size);\n      }\n      else {\n        failf(data, \"Operation timed out after %\" CURL_FORMAT_TIMEDIFF_T\n              \" milliseconds with %\" CURL_FORMAT_CURL_OFF_T\n              \" bytes received\",\n              Curl_timediff(*now, data->progress.t_startsingle),\n              k->bytecount);\n      }\n    }\n\n    /* Force connection closed if the connection has indeed been used */\n    if(data->mstate > MSTATE_DO) {\n      streamclose(data->conn, \"Disconnected with pending data\");\n      *stream_error = TRUE;\n
